[title] ALIEN: COVENANT

[author_marker] Screenplay

[author_marker] by

[author_names] John Logan

[source_marker] Based on the screenplays by

[source_names] Dante W Harper

[source_names] And

[source_names] Michael Green

[draft_date] November 20, 2015

[notes] "Better to reign in Hell than serve in Heaven."

[source_names] Milton
[source_names] Paradise Lost

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE

[action] Peaceful slow DARKNESS.

[action] Soon shattered by violence. The birth of a new star.

[action] Cosmic dust floats, moving faster now, spiralling into an 
[action] inner, spinning core. FLASHES like heat lightning, until the 
[action] hydrogen in the center IGNITES, bursting into BRIGHT FLAME--

[action] A SHOCKWAVE ripples out, CRASHING PAST US, OBLITERATING 
[action] EVERYTHING--

[action] Then we jump to a wider view.

[action] We see the vast explosion is a tiny puff, the new star among 
[action] many stars.

[action] All is still and quiet. The serene ocean of space.

[action] Until a TINY OBJECT whips past the edge of a nebula.

[action] We find it, closing in on...

[scene_heading] EXT. COLONY SHIP COVENANT

[action] The COVENANT.

[action] A COLONY SHIP; built for speed and distance. The STARS AROUND 
[action] HER DISTORT, gravity LENSING as the ship moves faster than 
[action] light.

[action] TITLES:

[action] Deep-Space Colony Ship COVENANT
[action] CREW: 14, +1 SYNTHETIC
[action] PASSENGERS: 3600
[action] MISSION: Terraform and Populate
[action] DESTINATION: Origae-6
[action] DATE: 2103, 239 light-years from Earth

[action] The COVENANT is 500 meters long. The long central body of the 
[action] ship is made of self-contained MODULES, like wagons in a  
[action] wagon train. Jutting from the central body are three dominant 
[action] areas: INTERSTELLAR DRIVE; CREW MODULE; TERRAFORMING BAY.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-VARIOUS

[subheading] THE NURSERY

[action] Like being inside a gigantic snow globe. A gentle SNOW falls. 
[action] Everything encrusted in a light frost.

[action] In the darkened PASSENGER CRYO-STASIS HOLD, a kiosk beeps, 
[action] awakens. NAMES SPELL OUT on a screen crusted in ice. Dim 
[action] green light flickers out over 3600 CRYO-PODS, under the thin 
[action] blanket of SNOW ... The thousands of pods sweep up along the 
[action] huge curved wall, defying gravity.

[action] We see the FROZEN FACES of... WOMEN, MEN, CHILDREN ... Also 
[action] banks of EMBRYOS at various stages...

[action] The kiosk finishes the inventory, the female COMPUTER VOICE 
[action] speaks:

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] 7 o’clock. All’s well.

[action] Ship’s BELL rings. The displays flicker out. All goes dark.

[subheading] THE BRIDGE

[action] EMPTY and DARK until... Blinders on the bridge windows OPEN. 
[action] DISTANT SUNLIGHT spills over captain’s chair.

[subheading] TERRAFORMING BAY

[action] A gigantic, shadowy chamber. Enormous VEHICLES. Earth-moving 
[action] trucks and machines. Clamped and tethered in position. Poised 
[action] and waiting like silent dinosaurs.

[subheading] CREW SLEEP BAY

[action] 14 HYPER-SLEEP PODS. Dancing readouts indicate the health 
[action] status of the sleeping CREW.

[action] We note something different about this sleep bay: all the 
[action] pods are set in pairs. Two by two, like Noah’s Ark.

[subheading] CORRIDOR

[action] We CREEP DOWN A LONG HALL, we hear... someone WHISTLING...

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-WARDROOM

[action] Large room, tables, cooking equipment. Where the senior crew 
[action] congregates. 

[action] One wall is a dramatic VERTICAL GARDEN that slopes up 
[action] elegantly from the floor. Fruits, vegetables, greenery for 
[action] oxidation. Hydroponic pools on various tiers.

[action] SOMEONE tends the garden, whistling. Back to us. On his 
[action] knees, planting a seedling.

[action] His hands are delicate, loving. This man cares about living 
[action] things.
[page_number] 2.

[action] He glances up as he wipes some condensation from his brow.

[action] Is it DAVID? The android from Prometheus?

[action] No. It’s WALTER. An android who looks identical to David. 
[action] Only his hair is dark and combed differently.

[action] We hear MOTHER, the computer:

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] That’s a fallacy, you know.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] What?

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] That music facilitates plant 
[dialogue] growth.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Why do you think I was whistling to 
[dialogue] the plant?

[action] He stands and cleans his hands. Proud of his work.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] It’s time to recharge the energy 
[dialogue] grid. Let’s be about it, Walter.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Nag, nag, nag.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] Mother knows best.

[action] He smiles, goes.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-CORRIDORS

[action] He moves through the corridors, playing with the little bit 
[action] of dirt on his fingers.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Do you not like whistling?

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] I like efficiency.

[action] He moves into...

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT - BRIDGE

[action] The lights flicker on as Walter moves to one of the control 
[action] consoles.
[page_number] 3.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] There’s got to be more to life than 
[dialogue] efficiency.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] Not for machines, Walter.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] (sotto)
[dialogue] Bitch.

[action] He settles into the controls and goes to work, keying in:

[action] INITIATE AUTOMATED REFUEL CYCLE

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - ORBITING ICE GIANT

[action] The GLOWING BALL OF ENERGY that powers the INTERSTELLAR DRIVE 
[action] dims, and the optical ripple around the ship settles, as the 
[action] CONVENTIONAL DRIVES FLARE into life.

[action] The COVENANT swings into orbit around the METHANE-BLUE ICE 
[action] GIANT. Then...

[action] The ship’s huge ENERGY COLLECTION SAILS UNFURL, silvery, 
[action] diaphanous...

[action] Now, the amazing ship is something new: beautiful. The 
[action] majestic sails give it the romantic feel of a mighty Galleon.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE - LATER

[action] The distant SUN and the GAS GIANT, the edges of COLLECTION 
[action] SAILS visible.

[action] Walter sits, feet up. Looking at that last bit of dirt on his 
[action] fingertips. He blows it away, something poignant and human in 
[action] the tiny act.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] Walter. We may have a problem ... A
[dialogue] strong neutrino burst was detected
[dialogue] in sector 106. It was weak, but it
[dialogue] could trigger a larger event...

[action] On the HOLO NAV DISPLAY Walter ZOOMS IN on the NEBULA, seeing 
[action] FLARES here, there...

[action] ...then a MUCH LARGER FLARE. His calm eyes suddenly anxious--

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Channel all reserve power to the 
[dialogue] magnetic shielding and retract the 
[dialogue] sails--
[page_number] 4.

[action] But then the DISTANT NEBULA EXPLODES into BLINDING LIGHT, the 
[action] bridge filling with a loud radio-electric HISS as outside--

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - IN ORBIT ABOVE GAS GIANT

[action] A massive SHOCKWAVE of CHARGED PARTICLES roils through the 
[action] system. The IONOSPHERE of the GAS GIANT ignites with 
[action] CRACKLING AURORAE as--

[action] The SHOCKWAVE SLAMS INTO THE COVENANT--

[action] THE COVENANT ROLLS VIOLENTLY-- 

[action] The sails on one side SHRED, BURN, as the entire ship lurches  
[action] dangerously--

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] Walter staggers to his feet, momentarily blinded. ALARMS are 
[action] sounding. The HOLO-MAP FLICKERS, WAVERS.

[action] But Walter is android-calm as he flips at high speed through 
[action] DAMAGE REPORTS on a HOLO MAP of the CRAFT--

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] Multiple system failures in need of 
[dialogue] attention, but overall structural 
[dialogue] integrity was maintained during the 
[dialogue] incident--

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I know, Mother. Please initiate 
[dialogue] emergency crew revival. And I’ll 
[dialogue] need--

[action] His eyes narrow on a BLINKING DAMAGE REPORT FROM THE NURSERY--

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Fuck.

[action] He bolts--

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-CORRIDORS

[action] Walter runs flat-out, no apparent exertion, an android’s 
[action] amazing mechanical speed--

[action] Meanwhile--

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-PRIMARY CREW SLEEP BAY

[action] DANIELS gasps painfully--
[page_number] 5.

[action] She bolts awake, slamming into the closed plastic lid of her 
[action] sleep pod--

[action] Her hands batter up at it as it slowly opens. She wrenches 
[action] herself up and out and into--

[action] Chaos.

[action] Most of the pods are already open. Others are opening. The 
[action] crew can be seen, kneeling, on the floor, puking, sweating 
[action] and shaking--

[action] Sparks and flashing emergency lights. Smoke. The din of sharp 
[action] klaxons--

[action] DANIELS is disoriented -- there’s someone shaking her--

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Daniels, we -- Can you hear me...?!

[action] ORAM -- intense, sternly commanding -- is at her side, he’s 
[action] little better than she is, sweating and ill--

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] For fuck sake, wake up! There was a 
[dialogue] power surge and--

[action] TENNESSEE -- her old friend -- helps pull her up--

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Come on, darlin’, Jacob’s in 
[dialogue] trouble. Jacob needs us--

[action] At that, she spins--

[action] The pod next to her, her husband’s pod, has been damaged--

[action] It’s crackling with sparks and smoking inside. Daniels claws 
[action] at it. She can see her husband’s face. He’s still asleep, but 
[action] twitching in pain.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] GET IT OPEN!

[action] Daniels and Tennessee wrench at the pod to open it. Oram
[action] tries all the electrical commands: sparks explode. Growing 
[action] panic. Her husband’s face twitches more in pain.

[action] SERGEANT LOPE quickly joins them -- he’s a soldier, muscular, 
[action] steady and responsible--

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] Stand back!
[page_number] 6.

[action] He SLAMS at the pod’s lock mechanism uselessly with an 
[action] emergency fire axe--

[action] Inside the pod, more sparks, and smoke--

[action] And then fire!

[action] Daniels flings herself on the pod, clawing desperately--

[action] Inside--

[action] Her husband’s eyes snap open.

[action] They lock eyes.

[action] For one moment.

[action] Everything is very still.

[action] Then--

[action] The pod is filled with flames. He’s incinerated before her 
[action] eyes. Under her hands.

[action] She SCREAMS.

[action] Tennessee wrenches her away from the pod and folds her into 
[action] his body, comforting.

[action] Oram and Sergeant Lope step back. Defeated, emotional. Oram
[action] sinks to the ground. His wife -- KARINE -- comforts him.

[action] Silence in the room, everyone watching.

[action] Then the pod’s fire retardant kicks in. Filling it with a 
[action] blast of steam. So we can no longer see the horror within.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-NURSERY

[action] Walter bolts in at top speed--

[action] Screeches to a stop.

[action] His worst fears realized.

[action] One of the sections of sleeping pods has COLLAPSED. Fallen in 
[action] on itself. A terrible image of sparks and shattered pods and 
[action] dead cold BODIES.

[action] Death has come to the Covenant.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-COMMUNAL SHOWERS/LOCKER ROOM - LATER

[subheading] IN THE SHOWERS
[page_number] 7.

[action] CREW MEMBERS shower in silence. Large room, unisex.

[action] Tennessee’s wife, FARIS -- every bit the engineer and pilot 
[action] he is, and equally big hearted -- shares a look with her 
[action] husband. What a fucking day.

[subheading] IN THE LOCKER ROOM

[action] OTHERS pull on mission jumpsuits, somber. Some STRETCH, sore, 
[action] still recovering from sleep. All anxious, tense.

[action] RICKS, bridge crew, looks out a window with his wife, 
[action] UPWORTH, also bridge crew.

[action] They see bits of a BURNED SAIL - micron-thin foil - hanging 
[action] tattered and still.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] How bad is it?

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] Looks like the electrics got it 
[dialogue] worst. Mostly stuff we can fix.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] (passing from shower)
[dialogue] Yeah, except for the stuff we 
[dialogue] can’t.

[action] He means...

[action] ONE MISSION JUMPSUIT hangs in an OPEN LOCKER, uncollected. 
[action] Names over the lockers: DANIELS and BRANSON. Photo of the 
[action] happy couple, and a NOTE, "WE MADE IT!"

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] After Jacob -- guess that makes 
[dialogue] Oram captain now, huh?

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Lucky us.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] Senior staff please assemble in the 
[dialogue] wardroom in ten minutes. Thank you 
[dialogue] for your promptness.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] (passing from shower)
[dialogue] Our master’s voice.

[action] She joins Tennessee at their lockers and they begin to 
[action] change.
[page_number] 8.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-WARDROOM

[action] The Senior Crew is gathered. As this is a COLONIZATION 
[action] MISSION the crew is made up of couples, to create stable 
[action] family units on their new world.

[action] We meet them again:

[action] ORAM: The new Captain. Life Sciences. Handsome, complicated 
[action] and ambitious, comes across arrogant.

[action] KARINE: his Wife. Life Sciences. Quirky and imaginative where 
[action] he is intense. Humanizes him a bit.

[action] TENNESSEE: Bridge crew and pilot. Daniels’ old friend. Rural 
[action] and wry. Solid as a rock.

[action] FARIS: Tennessee’s Wife. Bridge crew and pilot. His equal in 
[action] all things, and a better pilot. She wears a battered cowboy 
[action] hat.

[action] SERGEANT LOPE: Head of Security Team. Handsome, intense and
[action] committed. Old-school military, new-school relationship...

[action] He is joined by his male partner, SERGEANT HALLET, a Security
[action] Officer...

[character] SERGEANT HALLET
[dialogue] (to Oram)
[dialogue] Sorry I’m late.

[action] Lope gives Hallet’s hand a squeeze when he sits.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Captain.

[action] Hallet looks at him, unsure.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] I’m sorry I’m late, Captain.

[character] SERGEANT HALLET
[dialogue] Yes, sir. Sorry, sir.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] (to Walter)
[dialogue] Report.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] We lost forty seven colonists and 
[dialogue] 16 second generation embryos. And 
[dialogue] one crew member.
[page_number] 9.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] What the hell was it?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] A highly charged shockwave from a 
[dialogue] nearby stellar ignition. As the 
[dialogue] sails were deployed for the refuel, 
[dialogue] we absorbed the full brunt of the 
[dialogue] storm. If we had--

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] (interrupts)
[dialogue] What are the chances of something 
[dialogue] like this happening again?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Another such event would be highly 
[dialogue] unlikely. It was bad luck, sir.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] We’ve got, what, six more refuel 
[dialogue] cycles to go before we get there?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] If Origae-6 proves habitable, yes 
[dialogue] ... Shall we schedule the funeral 
[dialogue] services, sir? For the dead.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Let’s worry about repairs first.

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] (protests)
[dialogue] Hold on -- we just lost 47 
[dialogue] colonists -- and our Captain. We 
[dialogue] need to acknowledge that--

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] (to Oram, her husband)
[dialogue] He’s right, Bill.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] (snaps)
[dialogue] And if we don’t make repairs we 
[dialogue] could lose all the colonists -- and 
[dialogue] they are the entire point of the 
[dialogue] mission, in case you need 
[dialogue] reminding, ladies and gentlemen.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] We should do something for Captain 
[dialogue] Branson at least.
[page_number] 10.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] I really don’t think--

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] (firm)
[dialogue] No, we should do something for the 
[dialogue] Captain.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Well -- do we even know if he was 
[dialogue] religious?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] He was not.

[action] She’s just entered, joins them. Looks awful, on the edge, but 
[action] she’s there.

[action] DANIELS is a formidable woman. Tough, smart and beautiful. 
[action] There’s something dark and powerful in her, hidden depths.

[action] She’s fond of Walter and Tennessee is an old friend ... She 
[action] and Oram have never gotten along.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Hey, Danny. How ya doing?

[action] She sits. An empty chair conspicuous next to her.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Good, good. The terraforming module 
[dialogue] is stable although the, um, 
[dialogue] connecting strut took some damage. 
[dialogue] I still need to check the drones.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I can help if you like.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Thanks.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] How long before we can make our 
[dialogue] next jump?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Repairs should take a few days. But 
[dialogue] we should make an effort to vacate 
[dialogue] this sector, in case there are 
[dialogue] after-flares.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Then let’s go to work. Dismissed.
[page_number] 11.

[action] They disperse. Tennessee mouths to Faris: “ Dismissed ”? 

[action] Oram talks to Daniels privately.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] You should take a few days off.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I’d rather keep busy.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] That wasn’t a request.

[action] She looks at him evenly.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Look, Danny, I know Jacob and I 
[dialogue] didn’t always see eye to eye. But 
[dialogue] I’m responsible for the mission now 
[dialogue] -- and I need you at your best. 
[dialogue] Take a couple days. Cry it 
[dialogue] out,okay?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] How about I mourn in my own way? 
[dialogue] ... Sir.

[action] She goes.

[action] Oram stands for a moment, looking out the window.

[action] Is he up to the job?

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - “MORNING”

[action] Tennessee is working on the HULL of the ship, wearing a 
[action] heavyduty SPACE SUIT with its own propulsion systems. *

[action] He WELDS a blasted PANEL, arclight FLICKERING on his visor.

[action] The BLUE SUN crests, rising over the DARK GAS GIANT.

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Damn. Y’all should see this view.

[character] RICKS (ON COM)
[dialogue] We’re not seeing anything until you 
[dialogue] get the array fixed.

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Why don’t you look out the damn 
[dialogue] window, pal?
[page_number] 12.

[action] The MINI-JETS on his suit come to life, heading to the ship’s 
[action] prow. 

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-NURSERY 

[action] The light snow fall. The mist of frost.

[action] CAPTAIN ORAM stands, supervising a grim task.

[action] A Biohazard-suited team (PRIVATES LEDWARD and COLE) is 
[action] sorting through the mess of pale corpses, preparing them for 
[action] burial. Zipping them into body bags.

[action] It’s tough, physical work and Ledward and Cole fumble with 
[action] one of the bodies as they put it into a body bag.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] (snaps)
[dialogue] Some reverence for the dead, 
[dialogue] please.

[action] They exchange a look:  asshole .

[action] KARINE, monitoring some of the sleep pods, shoots a look to

[action] Oram:

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] Easy, Bill.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-DANIELS’ CABIN

[action] Too big for one really.

[action] She sits, hunched on the king size bed.

[action] Her fingers unconsciously touching her wedding ring as her 
[action] eyes move over the room... her husband’s shoes next to hers 
[action] in the closet... his collection of old vinyl RECORDS and 
[action] PHONOGRAPH.

[action] Her eyes stop at the pictures of them on the dresser.

[action] She goes to the dresser, starts removing his clothing...

[action] LATER:

[action] All his CLOTHING, neatly arranged around the room. She looks 
[action] at it all. Touching his things. Feeling him in them.

[action] She’s emotional now, can’t push it away anymore.

[action] LATER:
[page_number] 13.

[action] Photographs and holo-pictures of them. Spread out like a 
[action] mosaic on the floor. She kneels amidst them; studying them, 
[action] touching them, saying goodbye.

[action] She presses a HOLOGRAM-PICTURE activation button:

[action] Her late husband JACOB in front of the Grand Teton mountains 
[action] in Wyoming:

[character] JACOB (HOLO-PICTURE)
[dialogue] Hey, when are you getting here? I 
[dialogue] miss you! Look at those mountains. 
[dialogue] I know, I know, I said I wouldn’t 
[dialogue] climb without you but -- come on, 
[dialogue] look at that! Get your ass up here 
[dialogue] or I can’t promise--

[action] She freezes the image.

[action] Sobbing now.

[action] Her tears splashing through the hologram image, like rain.

[action] LATER:

[action] Everything has been put away. Neat and tidy.

[action] She stands in the center of the room.

[action] Her eyes red, exhausted from weeping. Absolutely no idea what 
[action] to do with herself.

[action] She jumps when the DOOR CHIME sounds.

[action] She answers the door, it’s WALTER. He carries a little box.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Good evening. Do I intrude?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] No. Come in. Good to see you ... 
[dialogue] Good to see anyone.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I brought you something.

[action] He hands her the box. She opens it. Inside: three perfectly 
[action] rolled joints.

[action] She smiles, glances to him.
[page_number] 14.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] The atmospheric conditions in the 
[dialogue] garden are ideal for cannabis 
[dialogue] growth.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] You think of everything.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] It’s just my programming.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] That’s not true.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] If I may ... I understand that 
[dialogue] keeping active can be an effective 
[dialogue] method in helping to process 
[dialogue] trauma. Would it be useful to go 
[dialogue] back to work?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Oram took me off the duty roster.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I wasn’t suggesting we inform him.

[action] She looks at him, amused, thankful.

[scene_heading] INT. TERRAFORMING BAY

[action] Flashlights cut through the icy gloom.

[action] The tires of the enormous earth-moving trucks tower above 
[action] DANIELS and WALTER. The cranes and diggers and dump trucks
[action] are mountainous. Strange vehicle shapes and silhouettes of 
[action] hanging equipment.

[action] This is Daniels’ domain. Her toys. She’s the mission’s Chief 
[action] Terraformist.

[action] She and Walter wear SPACE SUITS. They check the vehicles and 
[action] climb higher in the chamber as they talk.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] ... It wasn’t even my idea. Jacob 
[dialogue] had this dream of building a cabin 
[dialogue] on a new world -- like the old 
[dialogue] pioneers on Earth. A cabin next to 
[dialogue] a lake. There’s a lake in the 
[dialogue] terraforming zone on Origae-6. I 
[dialogue] mean a real cabin, made of real 
[dialogue] wood. So in ship’s stores there’s 
[dialogue] wood for a cabin. And metal nails. 
[parenthetical] (MORE)
[page_number] 15.

[character] DANIELS (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Which I don’t have the slightest 
[dialogue] idea how to assemble. I’m sure he 
[dialogue] threw away the instructions, 
[dialogue] because he was always doing that... 

[action] They stop for a moment as she checks some equipment. He 
[action] shines his flashlight to help her see.

[action] A beat.

[action] She stops. She looks down over the massive terraforming
[action] equipment. He sees the sadness creeping into her eyes.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] All of this, to make our new life 
[dialogue] ... Now I wonder, why bother?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Because you promised to build a 
[dialogue] cabin on a lake.

[action] She looks at him. Appreciates the sentiment.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] ... There’s one more thing I’d like 
[dialogue] your help with.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-WASTE EJECTION

[action] The ship’s disposal facility. Currently rigged for a grim 
[action] task.

[action] Her husband’s pod-like coffin is ready to be ejected.

[action] WALTER stands with DANIELS.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Would you like me to say something? 
[dialogue] I’m programmed with multiple 
[dialogue] funerary services in a variety of 
[dialogue] denominations, or nondenominational 
[dialogue] if you prefer.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] No, thanks.

[action] A beat.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] If you don’t need a funeral 
[dialogue] service, may I ask why you wanted 
[dialogue] me to accompany you?
[centered] DANIELS (CONT'D)
[page_number] 16.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] The crew is made up of couples. 
[dialogue] That was the whole point ... 
[dialogue] Everyone but me now.

[action] She looks at him.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I thought you might know something 
[dialogue] about being alone.

[action] He seems touched, in his way.

[action] The door slides open and TENNESSEE and his wife FARIS join 
[action] them. They have a bottle of Jack Daniels and four shot 
[action] glasses. Old friends.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] (hugs her)
[dialogue] Hey, Danny.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Thanks for coming.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] (re: the bottle)
[dialogue] His favorite. Tennessee’s finest.
[dialogue] Man with taste.

[action] He pours.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Straight up. “No ice, no water, no 
[dialogue] chaser, no shit” ... Walter?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] When in Rome.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] Amen, brother.

[action] They all have drinks.

[action] A moment.

[action] Tennessee toasts.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] A good man, gone too soon. Remember 
[dialogue] him.

[character] ALL
[dialogue] Remember him.
[page_number] 17.

[action] They drink.

[action] Daniels looks at the coffin one last time.

[action] Prepares herself.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Mother. Music database. Jacob jazz 
[dialogue] mix one. Track one.

[action] Nat King Cole’s “Unforgettable” begins to play. Her husband’s 
[action] favorite song. Tears in her eyes.

[action] She steels herself for it. Reaches forward. Presses a button 
[action] and--

[action] WHOOSH--

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT

[action] The coffin-pod shoots from the ship...

[action] Arcing away dramatically as the song swells...

[action] And is finally gone.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE - “MORNING”

[action] Next day. RICKS, UPWORTH and FARIS are at their stations. 

[action] TENNESSEE is again working in space. CAPTAIN ORAM and WALTER 
[action] enter:

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] Array’s almost on-line, sir.

[action] Oram warily sits in the Captain’s chair. Tests the feel of 
[action] it.

[action] Then the full LIGHTS begin to flicker on. All the consoles 
[action] spring to life. HOLOGRAMS flash and form and reform quickly, 
[action] orienting themselves. Relief from the crew.

[character] UPWORTH (TO COM)
[dialogue] Well done, T. We’re live up here. 
[dialogue] Come on in.

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Don’t leave without me now.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] Please, leave without him.

[action] DANIELS enters, goes to Oram:
[page_number] 18.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Request permission to return to 
[dialogue] duty, Captain.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Granted and welcome back. How you 
[dialogue] feeling?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] (glance to Faris)
[dialogue] Little headache.

[action] Faris smiles. “Little headache” means hung over.

[scene_heading] EXT. OUTSIDE THE SHIP - “MORNING”

[action] Meanwhile, TENNESSEE is working in a SPACE SUIT at the 
[action] farthest reaches of one of the newly-repaired sails; at the 
[action] distant communications satellite array.

[action] Various digital and telemetry READOUTS are reflected on the 
[action] inside of the visor of his helmet so he can monitor them. 

[action] He has completed his work. He closes a maintenance panel and 
[action] then maneuvers the jets on his suit to return to the ship: 

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Heading back to the airlock. Please 
[dialogue] have a cold one ready for the weary 
[dialogue] traveller. 

[character] UPWORTH (ON COM)
[dialogue] You’re still on duty, T.
[dialogue] (sotto)
[dialogue] And the Captain’s on the bridge by 
[dialogue] the way, so watch-- 

[action] SUDDENLY-- 

[action] The audio goes DEAD. And the digital displays SNAP OFF.

[action] Silence. Darkness but for the glow from outside.

[action] It’s now very quiet and dark and lonely inside that helmet. 

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] ... Covenant, you reading me...? 
[dialogue] Covenant, please respond... 

[action] Nothing. Then-- 

[action] A GHASTLY WHITE FACE SUDDENLY FLASHES PAST HIM-- 

[action] ON THE INSIDE OF HIS HELMET--
[page_number] 19.

[action] INCHES AWAY--

[action] HE SCREAMS IN SHOCK--

[character] UPWORTH (ON COM, BREAKING UP) 
[dialogue] Tennessee, we’re having trouble -- 
[dialogue] you -- 

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM) 
[dialogue] Ghost! Saw a fucking ghost!

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE - “MORNING”

[action] FARIS leans into Upworth’s station: 

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] What did he say? 

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] A ghost... 

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Tennessee, you reading me?

[scene_heading] EXT. OUTSIDE THE SHIP - “MORNING” 

[action] With Tennessee-- 

[action] AGAIN-- 

[action] The ghastly WHITE FACE, flicking across the inside of his 
[action] visor, clearer now.

[action] A WOMAN. In anguish. 

[action] The face still unrecognizable, stretching like ECTOPLASM-- 

[action] This time there’s a TERRIBLE SCREECHING SOUND -- deafening -- 
[action] terrifying -- a jumble of mad sounds -- like SPEAKING IN 
[action] TONGUES--

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] FUCKING GHOST! There’s a ghost out 
[dialogue] here. Get me inside! Get me inside! 

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE - LATER

[action] Tennessee’s HELMET is on a diagnostic table, connected to 
[action] various computers and monitors. 

[action] The crew is gathered around it. The helmet is projecting 
[action] telemetry and data into the air, a HOLOGRAM.
[page_number] 20.

[action] Then they see the anguished FEMALE FACE flickering through 
[action] the hologram-- 

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] There she is. 

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] What the hell is that?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] It’s a rogue transmission most 
[dialogue] likely. Your helmet must have 
[dialogue] picked it up because you were so 
[dialogue] far out, past our communication 
[dialogue] buffers.

[character] UPWORTH (AT HER STATION)
[dialogue] It’s a signal for sure. Coming from 
[dialogue] somewhere in the neighborhood. But 
[dialogue] nothing there on the charts. 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Mother, can we hear the audio, 
[dialogue] please? 

[action] The HOLOGRAM images pulse and shake as the AUDIO plays. The 
[action] SCREECHING FRIGHTENING sounds. 

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] Gotta be an echo. Or a glitch. The 
[dialogue] instruments took a lot of damage--

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] (pulling up data)
[dialogue] No. It’s in the logs, too. Every 8 
[dialogue] seconds, ever since we got here. 

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Speaking in tongues... 

[action] They look at him. 

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] I was raised Pentecostal. Real hell 
[dialogue] and brimstone. This sounds like 
[dialogue] speaking in tongues ... Mother.
[dialogue] Slow down the signal to discrete 
[dialogue] word patterns. And reverse it. 

[character] MOTHER 
[dialogue] Working. Please stand by.
[page_number] 21.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] The Devil’s tongue is God’s 
[dialogue] language inverted. The language of 
[dialogue] lies. 

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] I’ve reoriented the transmission, 
[dialogue] Captain Oram.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] On audio.

[action] A VOICE... 

[action] Still distorted and eerie. But human. Female. Urgent. 

[character] FEMALE VOICE (AUDIO BREAKING UP)
[dialogue] ... Please, please, if you hear 
[dialogue] this, come and... help us ... 
[dialogue] Everyone’s dying. They’re all 
[dialogue] dying... 

[action] They are stunned. A human. Out this far. 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] There’s geometric data, too. 

[action] Walter manipulates the FLOATING HOLO SIGNAL, eyes darting as 
[action] he decodes it, pulling the 3-D DATA around and connecting 
[action] points--

[action] Then the HOLO DISPLAY abruptly DISTORTS, FLICKERS, and GOES 
[action] OUT.

[action] SHAKY BLUE PIXELS suddenly FILL THE HOLO, SPILLING OVER INTO 
[action] THE BRIDGE, FILLING THE ROOM as-- 

[character] FEMALE IMAGE (HOLOGRAM)
[dialogue] ... Mountains of them now. I 
[dialogue] can’t... They’re all dying. It’s... 

[action] The IMAGE settles and morphs into a DISTORTED FIGURE, 
[action] WALKING, life-size.

[action] The image COHERES, and we can see... it’s ELIZABETH SHAW. 

[action] Her image FALLS APART, re-forms, as she PASSES through SOLID 
[action] OBJECTS...

[centered] SHAW’S IMAGE (HOLOGRAM)
[] ... Please come ... Please help 
[] us... We don’t know what... 

[action] SHAW’S IMAGE walks through a WALL, and they RUSH TO FOLLOW--
[page_number] 22.

[action] SHAW is in anguish now. She falls to her knees and prays. The 
[action] words of the Catholic prayer for the dead desperately 
[action] whispered-- 

[centered] SHAW’S IMAGE (HOLOGRAM)
[] ... “Eternal rest grant unto them, 
[] O Lord, and let perpetual light 
[] shine upon them, May the souls of 
[] all the faithful departed, through 
[] the mercy of God, rest in peace ... 
[] Eternal rest grant unto them, O 
[] Lord, and let perpetual light shine 
[] upon--” 

[action] The IMAGE abruptly LOCKS UP -- the pixels scatter, fading.

[action] A dumbfounded BEAT as the HOLO goes DARK, REBOOTS. 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] (eyeing his HOLO)
[dialogue] Interesting...

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] What?

[action] He turns to them. A sort of wonder in his eyes.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] It’s not coming from a ship.

[action] In front of him: a BLURRY NAVIGATION IMAGE of an UNCHARTED 
[action] STAR.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - LATER

[action] The SUN CRESTS the side of the GAS GIANT below. The COVENANT 
[action] is already looking ship-shape; half the sails up, none 
[action] burned.

[character] RICKS (V.O.)
[dialogue] She’s a main sequence star, a lot 
[dialogue] like our own. Five planets... 

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] ON THE HOLO CHART, fresh data showing a BRIGHT STAR and five 
[action] PLANETS.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] ...and one of them: square in the 
[dialogue] habitable zone. A prime candidate.

[action] He zooms in on a rough PLANETARY MODEL:
[page_number] 23.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] 0.96 Gs at surface, oceans. High 
[dialogue] likelihood of a living biosphere. 
[dialogue] Beyond our most optimistic 
[dialogue] projections for Origae-6, in fact.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Where is it?

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] She’s close. Just a short jump. We 
[dialogue] wouldn’t even have to go to sleep.

[action] The weight of this hits them all. No more hyper-sleep. A 
[action] possible new home.

[action] They all look to Oram. Time to be the Captain. 

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] All right, let’s take a look.

[action] LATER: 

[action] Daniels goes to Captain Oram at the forward window, where 
[action] he’s looking over the cosmos. She talks to him privately.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] You sure about this, Bill?

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] What do you mean?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I mean we spent a decade searching 
[dialogue] for Origae-6. We vetted it, we ran 
[dialogue] the simulations, we mapped the 
[dialogue] terrain -- it’s what we trained 
[dialogue] for.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] I understand that but--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] And now we’re going to scrap all 
[dialogue] that to chase a rogue transmission?

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] What does it cost us to take a 
[dialogue] look? A few weeks?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Think about it. A human being out 
[dialogue] here where there shouldn’t be any 
[dialogue] humans.
[parenthetical] (MORE)
[page_number] 24.

[character] DANIELS (CONT'D)
[dialogue] A hidden planet that suddenly 
[dialogue] appears out of nowhere. And a 
[dialogue] planet that just happens to be 
[dialogue] perfect for us. It’s too good to be 
[dialogue] true.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Look, I know you and Jacob had big 
[dialogue] plans about having a family on 
[dialogue] Origae-6 and--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] (protests)
[dialogue] Hold on, this has nothing to do 
[dialogue] with--! 

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] But what if this place is better? A 
[dialogue] better habitat for the colony--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] It’s irresponsible to deviate from 
[dialogue] the mission!

[action] It was too strongly stated. Too angry, too loud. She knows 
[action] it. People have glanced over.

[action] A beat.

[action] He gives her a patient, icy smile.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] You mustn’t question my judgement. 
[dialogue] Not on my bridge, Danny.

[action] He makes sure his words land, and then goes. 

[action] She gazes out the window, troubled.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-DANIELS’ CABIN - LATER

[action] She’s lying on the bed, smoking a joint. Deep in thought. 

[action] One of her late husband’s records spins on the old fashioned 
[action] turntable. Jazz plays.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] All crew members, please stand by 
[dialogue] for jump to interstellar drive. 
[dialogue] Thank you for your attention.

[action] She reaches over and lifts the needle from the record. Holds 
[action] it up. Doesn’t want to scratch the album in the jolt as--
[centered] DANIELS (CONT'D)
[page_number] 25.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - SPACE

[action] The Covenant's secondary drives goes DARK, and the 
[action] INTERSTELLAR DRIVE, like an artificial SUN, FLARES to life--

[action] The SPACE around the Covenant RIPPLES, and the Covenant 
[action] MOVES, slowly at first... then... she’s GONE--

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-DANIELS’ CABIN

[action] The stars bend and morph past outside the window. The ride is 
[action] utterly smooth now.

[action] Daniels lowers the needle back on the album. The jazz music 
[action] resumes.

[action] She lies back. Troubled.

[action] Takes a hit of the joint. Exhales.

[action] The smoke and the music taking us to...

[scene_heading] EXT. ENGINEER HOMEWORLD SUN - SPACE

[action] A BLUISH SUN flares in the vacuum of space ... The stars near 
[action] the sun RIPPLE as the COVENANT suddenly phases out of 
[action] hyperjump, sling-shotting around and slowing...

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT - BRIDGE-APPROACHING THE PLANET

[action] DANIELS is apprehensive.

[action] All hands at tense readiness as a WHITE-BLUE DOT, with TWO 
[action] MOONS, hangs in the distance...

[action] AT THE NAVIGATION HOLO, WALTER watches as Mother’s systems 
[action] flag the approaching planet and moons in real time...

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] (to Upworth)
[dialogue] You hearing anything?

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] Tried every band. It’s dead quiet 
[dialogue] out there ... Just the continuing 
[dialogue] signal from our friendly ghost.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] The siren’s song.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Bring us into close orbit.
[page_number] 26.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - APPROACHING THE PLANET

[action] The COVENANT cruises past a MOON, into HIGH ORBIT ABOVE THE 
[action] PLANET. The ship’s INSTRUMENT CLUSTER’S SCOPES ROTATE, 
[action] SCANNING, as--

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] The HOLO MODEL of THE PLANET REFINES as DATA PILES IN. 

[action] RICKS adjusts his view, changes spectra. He sees ARCING 
[action] TENDRILS of ENERGY around the planet--

[action] TENNESSEE frowns at it-- 

[character] TENNESSEE 
[dialogue] Hell of a strong ionosphere.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] Yep. Way better than Earth’s too. 
[dialogue] She’s lucky. Keeps good stuff in, 
[dialogue] and the bad stuff out; cosmic rays, 
[dialogue] solar flares - you name it. Like a 
[dialogue] big, warm blanket.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] Big warm blanket gonna be a 
[dialogue] motherfucker to land through.

[action] DATA MOVES on Walter’s screens.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I have visual.

[action] CLOUDS, OCEANS, CONTINENTS, what must be VEGETATION, shades 
[action] different than Earth - but richer ... A hush falls as they 
[action] watch.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] God damn. It’s just like Earth...

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] We’ll see, right?

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] What I wouldn’t give to feel a 
[dialogue] little ocean breeze, catch me some 
[dialogue] waves...

[action] As they SWING PAST THE DARK SIDE, DAZZLING GREEN AURORAE 
[action] float and drift, LIGHTNING flickers in storms. Continents... 
[action] DARK.
[page_number] 27.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] No lights. No visible signs of 
[dialogue] civilization.

[action] Oram doesn’t hesitate:

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Prepare the Lander.

[action] The crew, aside from Daniels and Walter, is delighted. High 
[action] fives and hollers.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - LATER

[action] The LANDER -- a large landing craft -- DISENGAGES from the 
[action] COVENANT.

[action] Its engines FLARE as it orients for DROP--

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] UPWORTH watches video feeds of the LANDER heading away.

[character] UPWORTH (ON COM)
[dialogue] Your trajectory looks gorgeous from 
[dialogue] up here, Faris.

[action] TENNESSEE, in command of the Covenant now, anxiously watches 
[action] through the forward window.

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER

[action] The LANDER READIES FOR ENTRY, already TINY AGAINST the VAST, 
[action] HALF-DARK PLANET, PEELING BY BELOW--

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-BRIDGE

[action] FARIS pilots the craft. CAPTAIN ORAM and WALTER are also 
[action] stationed on the bridge. The others are elsewhere in the 
[action] ship.

[action] TRAJECTORY MODELS tick by, the ship INVERTING as it DROPS.

[character] UPWORTH (ON COM)
[dialogue] Everything looking good down there?

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] All good, Covenant. Expect to hit 
[dialogue] upper atmosphere in five ... Might 
[dialogue] want to hang on in the back, we’re 
[dialogue] about to--

[action] The POWER on the Lander abruptly FLICKERS, DIMS...
[page_number] 28.

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Covenant? You read us?

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] An ALARM PINGS. Upworth frowns.

[action] Tennessee sees the LANDER’S ENGINES flutter OUT...

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-BRIDGE

[action] Faris hits UNRESPONSIVE CONTROLS, they DRIFT, ROLLING -

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] What’s going on...?!

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] We lost power.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Oh God--

[action] The ship starts to SHAKE VIOLENTLY from ENTRY. Faris’ tension 
[action] mounting, hitting MORE BUTTONS--

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-LANDING SEATS

[action] The other CREW MEMBERS are strapped in further back in the 
[action] ship. They grab on desperately.

[action] DANIELS shoots a look to SERGEANT LOPE. PRIVATE COLE crosses 
[action] himself. KARINE is very anxious at the turbulence:

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] Is this normal?!

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] No.

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] I hate space.

[action] Sergeant HALLET and Privates LEDWARD and ANKER complete the 
[action] landing party.

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-BRIDGE

[action] The ship is really DRIFTING NOW -- the TURBULENCE terrifying. 
[action] When suddenly--

[action] The LIGHTS flicker BACK UP. The engines GUN BACK ON -

[character] UPWORTH (ON COM)
[dialogue] - LANDER, DO YOU READ!?
[page_number] 29.

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Yeah, yeah. I read you. Think 
[dialogue] we’re... OK now.

[action] Then THEY’RE THROUGH, SLASHING THROUGH DENSE CLOUDS AND 
[action] SMOOTHLY CRUISING above DARK OCEAN that leads to MOUNTAINS...

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Underway - looks like we got 
[dialogue] mountains.

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER-MOUNTAINS - DAY

[action] The LANDER soars down, and slices through the clouds to 
[action] reveal...

[action] Majestic scenery. Slate grey mountains, tops obscured in 
[action] mist. Deep forests. Ansel Jacobs austere beauty. On the edge 
[action] of savage.

[action] The Lander HOVERS into a landing.

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-LANDING SEATS

[action] In the back, the EXPEDITION TEAM feel the ship SETTLE, and 
[action] all but tear off their seat belts, relieved.

[action] DANIELS is first up and out.

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-BRIDGE

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] What the hell happened up there?

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] New power cells must have fritzed
[dialogue] out. I’ll cycle ‘em while you’re 
[dialogue] gone.

[action] DANIELS enters, anxiously going to the windows.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] How far out are we?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Signal’s source is eight kilometers 
[dialogue] west. And up. Right on target.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] (nods out the window)
[dialogue] Walter, would you do the honors?
[page_number] 30.

[scene_heading] EXT. PLANET-BENEATH THE LANDER - DAY

[action] The LANDING BAY opens. 

[action] Walter walks down a ramp. Stops.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] One small step for man...

[action] He steps onto the planet’s surface.

[action] Inhales deeply. Takes a moment to analyze the air, even savor 
[action] it, like wine-tasting.

[character] WALTER (TO COM)
[dialogue] Atmosphere is 72% nitrogen, 23% 
[dialogue] oxygen. 40% humidity. 6% chance of 
[dialogue] precipitation ... All in all, 
[dialogue] lovely day for a stroll. 

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE - LATER

[action] TENNESSEE looks at the HOLO MODEL of the LANDER, the TERRAIN 
[action] spread out around it. DATA ERRORS prominent, parts of the 3-D 
[action] model’s surfaces BLOCKY and SHAKING...

[character] FARIS (ON COM, UNCLEAR) 
[dialogue] Not going to... suits... find the 
[dialogue] source of the transmission... 

[character] UPWORTH (TO COM)
[dialogue] Faris, see if you can get more 
[dialogue] power to the uplink, signal’s 
[dialogue] falling apart up here.

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-BRIDGE - DAY

[action] FARIS works various stations:

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] I’ll be cycling the power cells 
[dialogue] too, that might help.

[character] ORAM (ON COM)
[dialogue] Faris, we’re heading out. Keep 
[dialogue] expedition security protocols in 
[dialogue] place.

[action] She looks out the windows and sees...

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER - DAY

[action] ... CAPTAIN ORAM speaking up to her on com. She gives a 
[action] thumbs up in response.
[page_number] 31.

[character] FARIS (ON COM)
[dialogue] Understood. Have fun y’all.

[action] The EXPEDITION TEAM is ready. They have no need of breathing 
[action] gear, but wear properly tough expedition uniforms and packs.

[action] WALTER powers up a portable display - showing the way to 
[action] follow the signal.

[action] The SOLDIERS are heavily-armed.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] All right then. Let’s go find our 
[dialogue] ghost ... Walter?

[action] WALTER heads toward the treeline in the distance.

[action] Sergeant LOPE snaps open a beautiful folding BOW. Black 
[action] carbon fiber. He strings an arrow and leads the way ... His 
[action] men take up defensive perimeter positions.

[action] The expedition team is: Daniels; Walter; Captain Oram; 
[action] Karine; Sergeants Lope and Hallet; Privates Cole, Ledward and 
[action] Anker.

[scene_heading] EXT. FOREST-VARIOUS - DAY

[action] They walk, it’s quiet, footsteps muffled.

[action] All are alert. On edge. Silent.

[action] They travel ... The forest gets more dense, the terrain 
[action] rockier... up hill...

[action] They finally hear something new. Flowing WATER...

[scene_heading] EXT. FOREST STREAM - DAY

[action] They discover a beautiful FOREST STREAM, flowing down from 
[action] the distant mountains.

[action] And life.

[action] Midges and tiny INSECTS buzz about the water, like dust 
[action] motes, catching the light shafts slashing down through the 
[action] trees.

[action] It’s a bit of a miracle. Life.

[action] CAPTAIN ORAM smiles, his hand gently moving through the haze 
[action] of little insects.

[action] Suddenly SERGEANT HALLET jumps back, shocked--
[page_number] 32.

[character] SERGEANT HALLET
[dialogue] Fuck me--!

[action] A large SALAMANDER-like creature has run over his foot. 
[action] Disappears into some rocks. Sergeant Hallet laughs at his 
[action] scare ... Karine tries to find the salamander.

[character] PRIVATE COLE
[dialogue] How is this even... possible?

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] It’s not just possible - it’s 
[dialogue] predictable. Life is the result of 
[dialogue] very basic forces. Find a planet in 
[dialogue] the triple point, add sun and 
[dialogue] water. Thermodynamics starts the 
[dialogue] process - with a little luck, 
[dialogue] evolution does the rest.
[dialogue] (smiling)
[dialogue] But I’ll admit this is... beyond 
[dialogue] all expectations.

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Expedition team. You read us?

[scene_heading] INT. BRIDGE-COVENANT

[action] TENNESSEE can see the VIEW, the SUN dappling down, from the 
[action] team’s BODY CAMS. But the image is breaking up-- 

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] What’s happening? We’re having a 
[dialogue] hell of a time tracking you. You’re 
[dialogue] breaking up...

[character] ORAM (ON COM)
[dialogue] Almost halfway to target... life 
[dialogue] everywhere... Beyond anything...

[action] The signal dissolves into NOISE. The HOLO TERRAIN MAP 
[action] twitches.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Dammit.

[scene_heading] EXT. FOREST STREAM - DAY

[action] KARINE looks upstream, not wanting to leave.

[action] DANIELS eyes the sky, anxious.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] How much farther is it?
[page_number] 33.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] We’re about... five kilometers out, 
[dialogue] but it’s mostly uphill - a couple 
[dialogue] hours perhaps?

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] (to Oram)
[dialogue] Bill, I’d like to stay here. Might 
[dialogue] be our best chance to do a full 
[dialogue] ecology workup.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] (nods)
[dialogue] Sergeant, can you provide security?

[action] Sergeant Lope motions to PVT. LEDWARD, who joins Karine.

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] Ledward, stay with her. Meet back 
[dialogue] here in four hours. Keep your radio 
[dialogue] on.

[action] Captain Oram gives his wife a quick kiss.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Don’t touch anything icky.

[action] Karine smiles.

[action] The expedition team continues on.

[scene_heading] EXT. HIGH PLAIN - DAY

[action] An ethereal sight.

[action] Enormously tall grass, looks like wheat. Swaying beautifully 
[action] in the gentle afternoon breeze.

[action] The team is crossing a wide, high plain. Moving through the 
[action] sinuous grass that towers over them.

[action] Walter walks with Daniels.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Nice place for a log cabin.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Jacob would have loved this...

[action] Captain Oram catches up with them.
[page_number] 34.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] What do you think, Daniels? We 
[dialogue] could probably put the whole 
[dialogue] Covenant down here. Looks like a 
[dialogue] perfect landing site. Ready access 
[dialogue] to fresh water too... 
[dialogue] (gestures, imagining it) 
[dialogue] Put the housing modules over there, 
[dialogue] civic modules across the way. 
[dialogue] Instant colony! 

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] (non-committal)
[dialogue] We’ll see.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] We were meant to find this place.

[action] Daniels glances at him. Odd thing to say.

[action] Oram continues past them, trooping away, disappearing in the 
[action] swaying stalks.

[action] Daniels and Walter continue on through the jungle of tall 
[action] grass and come across LOPE and HALLET. 

[action] Lope has pulled down one of the long stalks and is crushing 
[action] the top in his hands, tastes a bit with the tip of his 
[action] tongue. 

[character] LOPE 
[dialogue] ... This is wheat. 

[character] DANIELS 
[dialogue] It can’t be. 

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] I’m from Iowa. Believe me, I know 
[dialogue] wheat.

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] How’s that possible? 

[character] HALLET
[dialogue] Maybe Casper will tell us... 

[action] Hallet whistles the “Casper the Friendly Ghost” theme song as 
[action] he and Lope continue on. 

[character] DANIELS 
[dialogue] (to Walter)
[dialogue] What are the odds of finding human 
[dialogue] vegetation this far from earth?
[page_number] 35.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Statistically -- it’s impossible. 

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] So... who planted it?

[action] They exchange a look and then continue on, pushing through 
[action] the high stalks. 

[action] We see there’s serious mountain terrain ahead. Sharp crags 
[action] and ridges. Much of this obscured by high elevation fog and 
[action] mist.

[scene_heading] EXT. FOREST STREAM - DAY

[action] Meanwhile, KARINE has filled a few mylar sample bags with  
[action] flora and fauna. Mostly plants and a couple of seemingly 
[action] uninteresting insects and worms. 

[action] She gently bats at a cloud of INSECTS in front of her face. A 
[action] haze of little mote-like MIDGES in the dappled sunlight.

[action] And we see one of the insects--

[action] MACRO VIEW:

[action] Ever-changing in its form -- lurching and stabbing into new 
[action] shapes -- oily black but beautiful ... It hovers, has 
[action] intelligence.

[action] Just before it can lunge at her--

[character] PVT. LEDWARD
[dialogue] (calls)
[dialogue] Hey, Doctor. You might want to see 
[dialogue] this.

[action] She joins him at the water’s edge.

[action] His back is to her. He turns. His face is covered in the tiny 
[action] mote-like INSECTS.

[action] By reflex she reaches out to brush them away.

[character] LEDWARD
[dialogue] (puzzled)
[dialogue] What?

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] You’re covered in midges.

[action] The insects evaporate in the sunlight.
[page_number] 36.

[action] Ledward, unconcerned, points to a little mammal, some kind of 
[action] furless thing. Half-eaten. Dead. Its side bubbles with fat, 
[action] white grubs.

[action] She kneels, looks at it, disturbed.

[character] PVT. LEDWARD
[dialogue] What is it?

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] No idea. But we know one thing now 
[dialogue] ... There are predators here ... 
[dialogue] Could you bag it for me?

[action] She moves away to use the field radio as Private Ledward bags 
[action] the dead creature. 

[action] And we focus on one of the INSECTS hovering near him... 

[action] MACRO VIEW: 

[action] It’s watchful, careful. It settles near Ledward’s ear, and 
[action] then enters the ear canal... the soft pink flesh... quickly 
[action] attaches itself -- a feeding tube plunging into his flesh-- 

[action] He unconsciously rubs his ear. Thinking nothing of it. 

[action] Meanwhile: 

[character] KARINE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Bill? Can you read me? ... Come in 
[dialogue] Expedition Party ... Lander? Faris
[dialogue] you reading me? Lander? ... Where
[dialogue] the hell is everyone?

[scene_heading] EXT. MOUNTAINSIDE - DAY

[action] The Expedition Team pushes through the FOG.

[action] It’s rocky and quite barren. Majestic. Difficult terrain, 
[action] slow going.

[action] SERGEANT LOPE leads. He’s in his element, bow ready, enjoying 
[action] the exertion. He’s more in shape than the others. They 
[action] struggle from the altitude and the time spent in hyper sleep.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Slow down, Sergeant, we’re not all 
[dialogue] soldiers...

[action] A PING! Walter stops, frowns at his NAV DEVICE -
[page_number] 37.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Says we’re right there. Doesn’t 
[dialogue] make sense.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Hold on ... Stop.

[action] Sergeant Lope gestures for his men to halt.

[action] Silence but for the wind. The breeze stirring the fog.

[action] Daniels is peering intensely... up.

[action] And then they can all see it. A LOOMING DARK SHAPE, now 
[action] clearer in the shifting mist ... ABOVE THEM...

[action] Massive overhanging rocks, protruding toward the sky like two 
[action] arms beseeching up to something.

[action] Daniels steps forward, then the rest, STARING UP, in silence, 
[action] at a looming, tubular arm of an ENGINEER DREADNOUGHT...

[scene_heading] EXT. CRASHED DREADNOUGHT - MOUNTAINSIDE

[action] Baffled, DANIELS frowns up at a... WALL? Curved. Huge. 
[action] Extending into the foggy mist. No one’s really sure what it 
[action] is yet.

[action] WALTER runs his hand along the curved, ridged surface. He 
[action] RAPS it with his knuckles, listening. Quite hollow.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I would say it’s... some kind of 
[dialogue] vehicle.

[action] Now the cloud-mist thins just enough to see one LONG, CURVED 
[action] ARM in its entirety. Jutting off the mountain at an angle.

[action] All are silenced by the majesty of the scale and design.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I don’t think she had a very good 
[dialogue] landing.

[character] PVT. ANKER (ON COM)
[dialogue] Think we found a way in, sir.

[scene_heading] EXT. DREADNOUGHT - OPEN DEPLOYMENT BAY - GLOOM

[action] The DARK RECESSES of the ship yawn beyond an open deployment 
[action] bay. They shine their flashlights into the CAVERNOUS SPACE.

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] Anker, Cole - stay on watch.
[page_number] 38.

[action] The two look happy to stay outside.

[action] SERGEANT LOPE snaps on the LASER SITE on his bow and leads 
[action] the way...

[scene_heading] INT. DREADNOUGHT - DEPLOYMENT BAY - GLOOM

[action] The group looks around, AWED by the vast alien hold. The wind 
[action] whistles through...

[action] They pick their way into the darker recesses. FLASH LIGHTS 
[action] and LASER SCOPES cut through the oppressive gloom.

[action] Underfoot the remains of many broken receptacles. BLACK URNS. 
[action] Scattered everywhere, empty, fragile shells. Crunching under 
[action] their boots.

[action] And they discover something else...

[action] EGG SHAPED OBJECTS.

[action] Randomly clustered. Different sizes. From aubergine to acid 
[action] green in color, abscesses ... None as large or impressive as 
[action] the originals we have seen in the past.

[action] They are growing out of a bed of BLACK FUR. Like the mold on 
[action] cheese. The black filaments gently sway as the air stirs 
[action] around them.

[action] SERGEANT HALLET leans close, studying an egg. Reaches a 
[action] finger out...

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] (shakes his head: don’t)
[dialogue] Hey.

[action] Hallet begins to follow the others out when--

[action] Wait -- was that movement? One of the eggs? Did it stir?

[action] Hallet remains fascinated by the movement. He goes closer...

[action] The eggs begins to open...

[character] SERGEANT HALLET
[dialogue] (to the others)
[dialogue] Guys....

[action] But they have already moved out of the chamber...

[action] His face is dangerously near the top of the egg...
[page_number] 39.

[scene_heading] INT. DREADNOUGHT - SLOPING HALLS

[action] They CLAMBER up into the HALLS.

[action] The dark ship isn’t level, and WATER DRIPS through the entire 
[action] thing, pooling in the corners. They splash through the halls.

[action] DANIELS eyes an OPEN SPACE JOCKEY SUIT. WALTER joins her. 
[action] Others stare, noticing OTHER SUITS. Rows of them. Not human 
[action] sized.

[action] Meanwhile--

[scene_heading] INT. DREADNOUGHT - DEPLOYMENT BAY

[action] HALLET peers very closely at the open mouth of the egg. Too 
[action] close.. 

[action] But then, he thinks the better of it and very sensibly takes 
[action] a step back... 

[action] But he inadvertently steps on a tiny egg behind him-- 

[action] Unleashing a cloud of the MIDGES. They waft up. Swirling 
[action] before him...

[action] MACRO VIEW:

[action] One of the black INSECTS darts forward -- into his nostril -- 
[action] the pink nasal passage -- attaches itself and stabs a feeding 
[action] tube into him--

[action] Hallet rubs his nose, unconcerned. 

[action] SERGEANT LOPE enters:

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] Tom, you okay?

[character] SERGEANT HALLET
[dialogue] Yeah, sure. Sorry.

[action] They go.

[action] Meanwhile--

[scene_heading] INT. DREADNOUGHT-NAV CHAMBER

[action] DANIELS, WALTER and ORAM explore the enormous NAVIGATION 
[action] CHAMBER. 

[action] Oram shines a light on EMPTY ENGINEER SLEEP PODS as Walter 
[action] investigates the old COMMAND CONSOLE.
[page_number] 40.

[action] There’s a pulsing LIGHT under one of the controls. Walter 
[action] activates it.

[action] Instantly, the HOLOGRAM message they saw before flashes to 
[action] life and begins to play quietly. SHAW kneeling, praying: 

[centered] SHAW’S IMAGE (HOLOGRAM) 
[] ... “and let perpetual light shine 
[] upon them, May the souls of all the 
[] faithful departed, through the 
[] mercy of God, rest in peace ... 
[] Eternal rest grant unto them, O 
[] Lord, and let perpetual...” 

[action] Walter steps straight through the HOLOGRAM then turns it off. 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Meet our ghost ... It’s a repeating 
[dialogue] message, non-directional. Like a 
[dialogue] holographic distress beacon. 

[action] Across the room, Daniels shines her flashlight-- 

[action] A GOLD GLINT catches her eye. On the floor. She reaches down, 
[action] discovering a GOLD CRUCIFIX NECKLACE.

[action] It’s Shaw’s old crucifix.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Over here.

[action] The others join her as Daniels finds...

[action] SHAW’S JOURNAL, lying in water. Daniels carefully picks it 
[action] up, opens the waterlogged journal. The few pages that aren’t 
[action] rotted together are illegible. But at front -

[character] DANIELS (READING)
[dialogue] Dr. Elizabeth Shaw.

[action] Oram finds the PHOTO of SHAW and HOLLOWAY smiling. 

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Is this her?

[action] Walter shines his flashlight, crisply registering the items 
[action] in the area. Decaying clothes. Some personal items.

[action] And then SHAW’S HELMET.

[action] He goes stock still when he sees the logo on the helmet...

[action] WEYLAND INDUSTRIES.
[page_number] 41.

[scene_heading] EXT. FOREST STREAM - AFTERNOON

[action] KARINE is trudging ahead, her specimen bags full as she 
[action] glances at the sinking sun, nervous.

[action] LEDWARD is moving slowly behind her.

[character] KARINE (ON RADIO)
[dialogue] Come in Lander One. Come in ... 
[dialogue] Come in Expedition Team. Are you 
[dialogue] reading me? Does any--

[action] Ledward doubles over, can’t get his breath.

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] Are you all right?

[action] He looks at her. He’s sweating profusely. Eyes red. Scared.

[character] PVT. LEDWARD
[dialogue] Not really... something...
[dialogue] (clutching his chest)
[dialogue] Can’t breathe...

[action] Then tiny droplets of BLOOD begin secreting from pores on his 
[action] face--

[action] Her face displays her shock, frightening him even more--

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] We better get you back to the 
[dialogue] Lander.

[scene_heading] INT. DREADNOUGHT-NAV CHAMBER - AFTERNNOON

[action] Captain ORAM and Sergeant LOPE are on the field radio across 
[action] the room. HALLET with them.

[action] Meanwhile, DANIELS and WALTER talk privately. He holds Shaw’s 
[action] HELMET.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Weyland Industries.
[dialogue] (off her look)
[dialogue] Sir Peter Weyland. Inventor, early 
[dialogue] 21st century. He created the first 
[dialogue] synthetic humans. He disappeared 
[dialogue] just over ten years ago ... And Dr. 
[dialogue] Elizabeth Shaw was chief science 
[dialogue] officer of the Prometheus, which 
[dialogue] also disappeared just over ten 
[dialogue] years ago ... Their mission was to 
[dialogue] discover the source of human life.
[page_number] 42.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] And it led them here...?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] As unlikely as it seems.

[action] Captain Oram calls from across the room:

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Come on. We have to go. We’ve got a
[dialogue] medical emergency.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] Tennessee is anxious, staring down at the planet.

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Are we talking about quarantine 
[dialogue] protocols?

[character] FARIS (ON COM)
[dialogue] I don’t know ... said ... he .... 
[dialogue] bleeding. And...

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] (snaps to Upworth)
[dialogue] Clean up the fucking signal!

[character] FARIS (ON COM)
[dialogue] ... Not sure what’s...

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-BRIDGE - LATE AFTERNOON

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] ... Do you read me? Covenant?

[action] Through the Lander’s front windows, she sees KARINE and 
[action] PRIVATE LEDWARD emerging through the trees. Karine’s helping 
[action] Ledward walk.

[action] Faris goes to meet them.

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER-LIFT BAY - LATE AFTERNOON

[action] FARIS comes out of the Lander’s OPEN LIFT BAY.

[action] KARINE is practically carrying LEDWARD, who looks much worse 
[action] now; head bowed, rag-doll, moaning.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] We have to get him to medbay. Now. 
[dialogue] Touch nothing on your way through. 
[dialogue] Follow me.
[page_number] 43.

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-MEDBAY

[action] A BLAST of light. The MEDBAY is high key white. White on 
[action] white.

[action] It’s barely controlled hysteria, quick and urgent.

[action] Faris pulls on gloves and a mask as Karine swiftly wrenches 
[action] off Ledward’s body armor.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] Put some fucking gloves on!

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] Little late for that.

[action] They HEAVE LEDWARD onto a table -- then Karine scrambles 
[action] through med cabinets, spilling supplies--

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] Karine! Stop touching everything!
[dialogue] I’ll do that!

[action] Karine drops a tray of medical supplies as Faris peels off 
[action] Ledward’s saturated shirt, revealing his back where--

[action] The skin on his spine is STRETCHING -- PARTING--

[action] Revealing a WET CROCHET of LIVING MATTER, like ALBINO WORMS 
[action] knitting together on his spinal column--

[action] Faris recoils - horrified.

[action] LEDWARD stares at her with VACANT, DEAD-EYED SHOCK--

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] We’re gonna get you up to the ship 
[dialogue] and get you fixed up - you just 
[dialogue] have to hold on for me, Private. 
[dialogue] Okay? Can you do that?

[action] Faris discretely backs out of the room: 

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] Stay with him. Keep him quiet. I’m 
[dialogue] going to contact the Captain.

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-OUTSIDE THE MED BAY

[action] Faris shuts the door and punches the keypad. The panel 
[action] FLASHES “LOCKED.”
[page_number] 44.

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] (anxious)
[dialogue] Captain? How long?

[character] ORAM (ON COM)
[dialogue] Hold on, we’re close.

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] I need you back here now.

[action] She hurries down the corridor.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] UPWORTH strains, FARIS’ FACE is dropping in and out on the 
[action] FEED--

[character] FARIS (ON COM BREAKING UP)
[dialogue] Expedition team’s not here and I 
[dialogue] ... the hell’s going on with 
[dialogue] Ledward...

[action] TENNESSEE is at the HOLO, seeing main team’s MOVING DOTS...

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Honey, calm down, just calm down -- 
[dialogue] Now tell me what happened?

[character] FARIS (ON COM BREAKING UP)
[dialogue] Calm down? Don’t tell me to calm 
[dialogue] the fuck down! You didn’t see what 
[dialogue] I ... no goddamn idea what’s 
[dialogue] happening...

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Honey, you’re breaking up. Can you 
[dialogue] read me...?

[action] IT GARBLES. OUT.

[action] He tosses his headset away angrily. 

[action] UPWORTH is standing at the HOLO-NAV DISPLAY... has an idea.

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] We need to move the ship...

[action] Upworth points out the idea on the HOLO-NAV DISPLAY:

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] Mother. If we drop to low orbit, 
[dialogue] we’ll be inside the field. We can 
[dialogue] cut the scatter at its source and 
[dialogue] clean up the signal, right?
[page_number] 45.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] The nature of the ionosphere is 
[dialogue] unknown to us. This course of 
[dialogue] action would be inadvisable without 
[dialogue] further information.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Well, can you speculate?

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] That’s not within my programming.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] We have to do something...

[action] Ricks and Upworth stare at Tennessee.

[action] Silence.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Fuck it. Bring us to 400 clicks 
[dialogue] from the planet surface.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - HIGH ORBIT

[action] The COVENANT’S PRIMARY DRIVES COME TO LIFE, the ship 
[action] ROTATING, and now MOVING CLOSER TO THE PLANET...

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] ON UPWORTH’S MONITORS: FARIS’ FEED, as well the HOLO MODEL, 
[action] comes into SHARPER, and CLEARER FOCUS...

[character] UPWORTH (TO COM)
[dialogue] Lander One, do you copy? Faris, 
[dialogue] come in.

[action] The planet CRUISING BY BELOW, looming larger... larger...

[character] FARIS (ON VID)
[dialogue] Yeah, better. What’d you do?

[character] UPWORTH (TO COM)
[dialogue] We moved the Covenant in low orbit.

[character] FARIS (ON VID)
[dialogue] Well it’s a good goddamn thing, 
[dialogue] because I think we’re gonna be on 
[dialogue] our way up pretty soon. We’re gonna
[dialogue] need-- 

[action] The feeds FLICKERS. The lights DIM, RHYTHMIC.
[page_number] 46.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Mother? Bridge is losing power, 
[dialogue] what’s going on?!

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] A fluctuation in thhhhhhhhhh -

[action] A SQUELCHING SQUEAL plays on the ship's COM, EARSPLITTING, 
[action] and then... the WHOLE BRIDGE GOES DARK.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Mother?

[action] NO ANSWER. Tennessee BOLTS UP, looking out the WINDOW, seeing 
[action] as... the REST OF THE COVENANT flickers... and GOES DARK.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Whole ship just went dark.

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-BRIDGE - SUNSET

[action] Outside, sun’s going down. Blood red sunset.

[action] FARIS turns to a vid-feed from the MED BAY: Karine is trying 
[action] to hold down LEDWARD, who is now CONVULSING violently--

[action] Karine calls up at the vid-feed camera--

[character] KARINE (ON VID)
[dialogue] FARIS! I NEED YOUR HELP!

[action] Faris bolts--

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-MED BAY/CORRIDOR - SUNSET

[action] FARIS runs to the MED BAY doors, looks through the porthole--

[action] LEDWARD is now sitting quietly on a MED TABLE, hunched, his 
[action] back to us.

[action] KARINE goes to the door, in shock.

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] I don’t know what’s wrong with him. 
[dialogue] He was-- 

[action] She tries the door, but Faris locked it previously. 

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] Let me out.

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] I’m sorry, I can’t do that.
[page_number] 47.

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] Faris -- let me out of here!

[action] This is agonizing for Faris.

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] Please.

[action] Faris fights not to panic. Tries to keep her voice steady: 

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Covenant, we need to quarantine the 
[dialogue] dock bay before we come back up, we 
[dialogue] have a medical emergency...
[dialogue] (beat)
[dialogue] Covenant? Come in.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE - DARKNESS

[action] The ship is groaning as it drifts.

[action] The only light on their faces is from the HALF-DARK PLANET 
[action] below.

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] Mother. How long before the backup 
[dialogue] systems come online? ... Mother?

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Mother died ... Backup’s automated. 
[dialogue] Should’ve kicked in by now.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] Jesus -- if we lost backup power 
[dialogue] what about the Nursery?!

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Yes! Go!

[action] Ricks goes quickly.

[action] Then a CREAKING echoes through, STRAINING METAL, followed by 
[action] gunshot-loud CRACKS! TENNESSEE'S EYES GO WIDE -

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Artificial gravity’s cycling out...

[action] As the last VIBRATIONS shake the ship, a CUP OF COFFEE SLIDES 
[action] off the console... and is AIRBORNE. FLOATING UP. Liquid 
[action] CURLING OUT as it lazily CLUNKS against the VIEWING WINDOW.

[action] Silence.

[action] Ricks -- sprinting -- is suddenly floating -- as if on water.
[page_number] 48.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Whatever this field is... we’re 
[dialogue] sure as hell in it now.

[action] Everything floats.

[scene_heading] EXT. FOREST - SUNSET

[action] The Expedition Team is moving quickly. Almost night now. All 
[action] exhausted. They can see the Lander’s lights in the far 
[action] distance.

[action] Suddenly--

[action] SERGEANT HALLET doubles over -- falls. Lope goes to him, 
[action] concerned.

[character] HALLET
[dialogue] Sorry -- I can’t--

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-MED BAY/CORRIDOR - SUNSET

[action] KARINE is at the door, desperate now--

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] Let me out of here!

[character] FARIS
[dialogue] You know I can’t do that--

[action] Faris is in tears. So wants to help her friend.

[action] Suddenly, his back still to the door, LEDWARD sucks in a 
[action] rattling BREATH -- his hands grip the seat, agony as he 
[action] convulses--

[action] Karine goes to him--

[character] KARINE
[dialogue] Shhh. You’re gonna be okay... I’m 
[dialogue] with you--

[action] Ledward’s convulsions increase -- she tries to hold him as 
[action] his body WRITHES, SEIZING, GRIPPED in the first PAINFUL 
[action] CONTRACTION.

[action] FARIS watches in horror through the porthole as suddenly--

[action] Between Karine’s hands--

[action] TWO GREY-WHITE SPIKES PUNCTURE LEDWARD’S BACK AND RIB CAGE--

[action] And then his ENTIRE BACK RUPTURES -- his rib cage splits--
[page_number] 49.

[action] BLOOD EXPLODES OVER KARINE and she SCREAMS AS--

[action] A NEOMORPH TEARS ITSELF OUT OF LEDWARD’S BODY.

[action] Vision from Hell. Emerging backwards. Small at first, about 
[action] the size of a large cat. Humanoid ... ELONGATED HEAD -- just 
[action] hinting at the original Alien -- and three SPIKES protruding 
[action] from the back. Dripping. Sickly pale WHITE FLESH.

[action] And growing quite fast. Jerking its spine bizarrely. Becoming
[action] itself.

[action] All the more terrible in the high key white room. The black 
[action] blood splattering on the white walls, ceiling and floor.

[action] LEDWARD collapses, dead, arched awkwardly backward across the 
[action] bed-- 

[action] KARINE -- covered in blood -- backs up in terror to a corner-- 

[action] As the MONSTROUS THING WHIRLS, seeing her, and with sickening 
[action] CURIOSITY, LOOKS at her--

[action] FARIS watches in horror through the porthole--

[action] Karine is SHAKING IN TERROR. Sinks to her knees. Afraid to 
[action] look up-- 

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-CORRIDORS - SUNSET

[action] FARIS spins and runs. RUNS SO FAST that she SLIDES, WIPING 
[action] OUT, PICKS HERSELF UP and continuing as:

[character] FARIS (TO COM)
[dialogue] THIS IS LANDER ONE! WE HAVE AN 
[dialogue] EMERGENCY!

[scene_heading] EXT. DARK FOREST - SUNSET

[action] The team marches quickly through the forest, exhausted. 
[action] Nearly full darkness now. The lights from the Lander closer.

[character] FARIS (ON COM, BREAKING UP)
[dialogue] SOMETHING GOT ON BOARD. SOME KIND 
[dialogue] OF... ANIMAL... HOSTILE. KILLED 
[dialogue] LEDWARD...

[character] ORAM (TO COM)
[dialogue] What?! Faris, repeat ... Come in 
[dialogue] Lander One... 

[action] They run now--
[page_number] 50.

[action] Sergeant Lope helps Hallet along, but Hallet is really 
[action] suffering--

[action] The Lander is almost visible through the trees now, they’re 
[action] almost there--

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER - LIFT BAY - SUNSET

[action] FARIS runs in-- 

[action] Rips open a WEAPON’S LOCKER-- 

[action] Grabs a weapon and runs-- 

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-MED BAY/CORRIDOR - SUNSET 

[action] FARIS approaches the MED BAY DOOR. Looks in: 

[action] Sees the NEOMORPH. Bigger now. Size of a CHIMP. Moving across 
[action] the room dangerously toward where KARINE is cowering--

[action] It ATTACKS-- 

[action] Faris can’t see what happens, it’s just out of her field of 
[action] vision through the porthole. She sees a SPRAY of blood. And 
[action] the SOUND of the attack is terrible: Karine’s SCREAMS; the 
[action] SCREECH of the NEOMORPH. 

[action] Faris readies her weapon and punches the door button-- 

[action] It slides open-- 

[action] She steps into the Med Bay, weapon ready-- 

[action] Can finally see-- 

[action] The NEOMORPH is sitting on Karine’s chest. Eating her face. 

[action] It SPINS TO FARIS--

[action] FARIS FIRES -- nothing -- Safety catch on! Fuck! 

[action] The NEOMORPH immediately attacks-- 

[action] FARIS bolts back through the door and slams the button-- 

[action] The door slides shut, just in time-- 

[action] FARIS runs in panic as the NEOMORPH pounds and slashes at the 
[action] door behind her-- 

[action] The door begins to buckle--
[page_number] 51.

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-CORRIDORS - SUNSET

[action] FARIS runs in absolute panic-- 

[action] Can hear the NEOMORPH in pursuit-- 

[action] She bolts into-- 

[scene_heading] INT. LANDER-LIFT BAY - SUNSET

[action] FARIS backs up in shocked terror, the WEAPON shaking in her 
[action] hands--

[action] She flicks off the safety catch-- 

[action] As the NEOMORPH -- LARGER NOW -- streaks in, darting around 
[action] the room-- 

[action] Moving past the OPEN WEAPONS LOCKER as-- 

[action] Faris FIRES-- 

[action] The NEOMORPH launches itself out of the Lander to safety -- 
[action] as her SHOTS spray across the OPEN WEAPONS LOCKER--

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER - NIGHT

[action] ORAM and the others are just clearing the trees in the 
[action] distance when--

[action] BLAM!

[action] A MASSIVE EXPLOSION as the Lander is destroyed--

[action] Just as--

[action] SERGEANT HALLET finally collapses, his back heaving WILDLY--

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] TOM--!

[action] DANIELS grabs SERGEANT LOPE, pulls him away from his partner--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] NO! STAY AWAY FROM HIM!

[action] WALTER helps hold SERGEANT LOPE back--

[action] Meanwhile, ORAM advances on the burning Lander in absolute 
[action] shock.

[action] He sinks to his knees, overcome. 

[action] He knows his wife is dead somewhere in that inferno.
[page_number] 52.

[action] Then, horribly--

[action] He sees FARIS emerge from the blaze, crawling, dying, hands 
[action] grasping -- finally collapsing in a terrible blackened, 
[action] burning mass-- 

[action] As we see SOMETHING WHITE -- the fully grown NEOMORPH -- 
[action] streak past him in the darkness-- 

[action] Back with the others: 

[action] HALLET CONVULSES--
