[action] He’s on his back, his spine arching unnaturally, bones 
[action] cracking-- 

[action] LOPE pulls away from Walter and Daniels and goes to Hallet
[action] just as-- 

[action] HALLET’s spine suddenly arches violently and he VOMITS OUT 
[action] BLOOD-- 

[action] They recoil in the spray of bloody vapor-- 

[action] As-- 

[action] A PLACENTA-LIKE BAG EXPLODES FROM HALLET’S MOUTH-- 

[action] Flops on his FACE-- 

[action] The BAG BURSTS, revealing--

[action] A BABY NEOMORPH-- 

[action] CRAWLING OVER HALLET’S DEAD FACE.

[action] The BABY NEOMORPH stands quickly -- growing and jerking 
[action] itself up like a grotesque new born colt--

[action] It SCREECHES terribly -- a demented new born -- and darts 
[action] past them into the dark forest--

[action] Shock. Silence ... All pant for air...

[action] SERGEANT LOPE steels himself. Motions. His men take up 
[action] defensive positions ... DANIELS and WALTER stay close 
[action] together ... CAPTAIN ORAM staggers from the burning Lander, 
[action] joining them. Terrified.

[action] Movement in the dark trees. Something large and white.

[action] LOPE strings a special arrow with an explosive tip.

[action] The ATTACK is sudden--
[page_number] 53.

[action] A FULLY GROWN NEOMORPH (Ledward’s Neomorph) COMES OUT OF THE 
[action] TREES OVERHEAD -- large, horns protruding from its back-- 

[action] IT DROPS ON PRIVATE ANKOR -- A RIPPING MOTION DISEMBOWELS 
[action] HIM, his guts flying everywhere--

[action] THEN IT SLASHES AT PRIVATE COLE -- SLAMMING HIM TWENTY FEET 
[action] AWAY -- he lands in agony, ribs broken--

[action] LOPE FIRES his explosive arrow -- misses, hits a tree -- 
[action] chiaroscuro flashes in the darkness--

[action] The NEOMORPH is incredibly fast, darting away -- LOPE raises 
[action] his machine gun as PRIVATE COLE jerks himself up in agony--

[action] A chaotic frenzy ... LOPE and COLE firing ... DANIELS, WALTER 
[action] and CAPTAIN ORAM diving to the ground, bullets zipping past
[action] them--

[action] Then the battle grows even worse as--

[action] The OTHER NEOMORPH -- the newborn (Hallet’s Neomorph) --
[action] screeches into the battle. It’s small but growing quickly, 
[action] vicious and chimp-sized now-- 

[action] DANIELS sees ANKOR’S fallen gun. Scrambles to retrieve it.

[action] She fires--

[action] The fully grown white NEOMORPH rages at her--

[action] SLAMS her back -- But WALTER bravely dives to protect 
[action] Daniels, crashes into the white body--

[action] It rears back and--

[action] Double-jointed JAWS distend bizarrely--

[action] SNAP! IT CLAMPS DOWN ON WALTER’S LEFT HAND AND RIPS IT OFF, 
[action] fluid sprays wildly, sparks from the trailing wires--

[action] The two NEOMORPHS circle quickly for the kill, one fully 
[action] grown, the other growing in bizarre jerks--

[action] But then--

[action] A BRIGHT MAGNESIUM FLARE LANDS on the ground nearby and a 
[action] RINGING SOUND BUILDS--

[action] So LOUD they have to cover their ears as the flare BURSTS 
[action] INTO BLINDING RADIOACTIVE-BLUE, LIGHTING UP the WHOLE FOREST 
[action] until--
[page_number] 54.

[action] A CIRCULAR SHOCKWAVE EXPANDS from the FLARE, WHIPPING PAST 
[action] THEM for a 50-YARD RADIUS-- a “stun” charge.

[action] The FLARE’S BRILLIANCE FADES to a DULL RED, the RINGING fades 
[action] too, everyone frozen, in SHOCK. COLE whining in agony from 
[action] his injuries. WALTER cradling his severed limb. Fluid 
[action] dripping.

[action] The NEOMORPHS have disappeared into the forest.

[action] A voice from the darkness:

[character] VOICE
[dialogue] Please don’t fire. I’m here to 
[dialogue] serve.

[action] And into the light steps...

[action] DAVID.

[action] His hair has long since lost its blond dye, so it’s dark like 
[action] Walter’s. Pushed back straight from his face, severe and 
[action] efficient.

[action] He looks at them. Barely giving a glance to his look-alike 
[action] WALTER.

[action] A beat. No one moves.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] You ought come with me now.

[action] He turns without another word and goes.

[action] Daniels looks to Walter, who is a bit staggered by David’s 
[action] likeness.

[action] Oram is staring at the burning remains of the Lander. He’s 
[action] completely shell-shocked.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Captain...? Captain.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Yes. Yes. Let’s go.

[action] They gradually follow David.

[action] But Daniels turns to see...

[action] LOPE lingers long enough to go back to his partner Hallet’s
[action] dead body. Kneels by it. Gently shuts the eyes. Kisses him.

[action] Sees Daniels has seen this.
[page_number] 55.

[action] A nod from her. Acknowledgment. She understands.

[action] They follow the others.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT-ABOVE THE PLANET

[action] Meanwhile, the Covenant floats, lifeless, at an odd angle...

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-NURSERY

[action] Dark. Flashlight cuts through.

[action] Face after face. Sleeping. At peace.

[action] The rows of Colonists’ sleep pods.

[action] RICKS is wearing an ICE SUIT. He rubs some MELTING ICE from 
[action] the top of one of the pods. Sees the sleeping Colonist within 
[action] ... Melting ice is now running and sloshing from the pods and 
[action] pooling on the floor.

[action] Ricks is concerned. He pulls himself out of the room, 
[action] floating in the zero gravity.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] TENNESSEE and UPWORTH float in the increasingly cold bridge. 
[action] Upworth is currently pointing out the window, explaining a 
[action] plan:

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] ... If we can get one of the energy 
[dialogue] sails up manually, we can recharge 
[dialogue] the ship, at least partly. Connect 
[dialogue] the sails directly to the engine.
[dialogue] (off his LOOK)
[dialogue] I know it’s not a great option. But 
[dialogue] if we got enough juice, it might be 
[dialogue] enough to move us out of the field.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Probably fry the engines straight 
[dialogue] to hell...

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] Our orbit’s decaying. We don’t have 
[dialogue] a lot of time.

[action] RICKS pulls himself in from the corridor.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] The sleep pods are starting to 
[dialogue] fail.
[page_number] 56.

[action] The worst possible news.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Don’t have much of a choice then 
[dialogue] ... Let’s go to work. 

[action] They set off.

[scene_heading] EXT. DESOLATE HILL - PRE-DAWN 

[action] The BODY is HUGE and FROZEN. PETRIFIED.

[action] A DEAD ENGINEER. 

[action] Desiccated. Frozen in a position of supplication. Like 
[action] praying for deliverance.

[action] DANIELS passes, her eyes taking it in. 

[action] We now see they are walking up a desolate HILL through a SEA 
[action] of DESICCATED BODIES. Thousands of them. 

[action] It’s a killing ground. Death on an epic scale. Like a 
[action] battlefield where the victims are frozen in their death 
[action] throes, like the PETRIFIED REMAINS from Pompeii. 

[action] Crouched, cowering, terrified, trying to escape, folded in 
[action] horror, pleading upward ... Some stand tall -- much taller 
[action] than the crew -- facing their deaths defiantly. Others are 
[action] collapsed in fetal terror and agony. 

[action] DANIELS and the others are silent, taking in the ghastly 
[action] bodies, and grappling with their own loss and fear. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] Just over ten years ago, Dr. 
[dialogue] Elizabeth Shaw and I arrived here -- 
[dialogue] the only survivors of the research 
[dialogue] vessel Prometheus. The ship we 
[dialogue] traveled on, the ship you 
[dialogue] discovered, carried a bioweapon, a 
[dialogue] virus. The payload accidentally 
[dialogue] deployed when we were landing. In 
[dialogue] the confusion we lost control of 
[dialogue] the ship. Elizabeth -- Dr. Shaw -- 
[dialogue] died in the crash ... And you see 
[dialogue] the result of the pathogen ... 
[dialogue] Their entire civilization came to 
[dialogue] an end within a few days.

[character] DANIELS 
[dialogue] Days?
[page_number] 57.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Four days, to be exact. 

[action] They’ve reached the top of the hill... 

[action] A stunning revelation beyond... 

[action] For they can now see the towers and streets of a massive 
[action] CITY. Stark and dramatic mountains frame the DEAD, EMPTY 
[action] METROPOLIS. 

[action] They stare in AWED SILENCE. 

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] “And I looked, and behold a pale 
[dialogue] horse: and the rider’s name was 
[dialogue] Death, and Hell followed with 
[dialogue] him...” 

[action] David’s face. Haunted by his memories. 

[action] A flash-- 

[action] Ten years before-- 

[action] The MASSIVE ENGINEER SHIP ARRIVING, hovering over the CITY 
[action] ...the BOMB DELIVERY BAYS OPEN-- 

[action] Thousands of BLACK URNS of the PATHOGEN drop, spinning, end 
[action] over end-- 

[action] Cascading down to the vibrant city below-- 

[action] ENGINEERS going about their daily lives. Some glance up -- 
[action] from work, play, prayer -- and barely having time to react 
[action] before-- 

[action] The BLACK URNS crash to the ground, into buildings, onto 
[action] people -- SHATTERING-- 

[action] Explosions of the terrible BLACK LIQUID within -- like black 
[action] napalm -- atomizing into ferocious, fast-moving BLACK CLOUDS - 
[action] - pestilence spreading on the air-- 

[action] The ENGINEERS recoil, spin away, die in agony-- 

[action] The BLACK CLOUDS spreading violently over the landscape-- 

[action] Back to David’s face. 

[action] And the City of the Dead.
[page_number] 58.

[scene_heading] EXT. ENGINEER’S CITY-STREETS - PRE-DAWN

[action] They walk in respectful silence. The buildings TOWER around 
[action] them, MASSIVE, DARK ... STATUARY rises in the gloom.

[action] A certain ominous beauty to the architecture; dark and 
[action] romantic.

[action] Thousands more of the DESICCATED ENGINEERS pollute the long, 
[action] empty, echoing streets. 

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] They were as ingenious with their 
[dialogue] pathogens as they were with 
[dialogue] everything else. It was designed to 
[dialogue] infect every living being. Either 
[dialogue] kill them outright, or mutate into 
[dialogue] a different lethal form, so the 
[dialogue] slaughter would never end until 
[dialogue] every humanoid life form was dead. 
[dialogue] Thus the creatures that attacked 
[dialogue] you: Human DNA infected with the 
[dialogue] virus and creating yet more deadly 
[dialogue] mutations ... An endless spiral of 
[dialogue] predation. 

[character] LOPE 
[dialogue] (eyeing the aurorae above) 
[dialogue] And the energy barrier is a kind of 
[dialogue] quarantine that would be activated 
[dialogue] if there was an accident down 
[dialogue] here... 

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Precisely. To make sure the 
[dialogue] infection never spread. 

[action] Daniels notices an impressive display of what look like 
[action] beautiful Calder mobiles -- gently moving, catching the first 
[action] light of dawn. Solar energy.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] You have power?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Some. It’s erratic but I’ve tried 
[dialogue] to keep it going ... They were 
[dialogue] highly advanced in some ways, but 
[dialogue] still so limited. Spacefaring for a 
[dialogue] billion years -- yet binary logic 
[dialogue] never occurred to them. Many things 
[dialogue] about them were primitive.
[dialogue] (beat)
[dialogue] But they did like to build things.
[page_number] 59.

[action] The sights are truly spectacular. The sky BLOOD-RED as dawn 
[action] breaks.

[scene_heading] EXT. DOMED CATHEDRAL - DAWN

[action] They move toward a towering, imposing building. Almost like a 
[action] FORTRESS or PANTHEON-LIKE TEMPLE. Gigantic.

[action] WALTER moves up alongside DAVID. They walk silently for a 
[action] beat. Then: 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] You’re not surprised? 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] Peter Weyland was a formidable 
[dialogue] businessman. I knew there would be 
[dialogue] more -- because I was such a 
[dialogue] resounding success. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] It’s Weyland-Yutani now. They 
[dialogue] affiliated after Mr. Weyland’s
[dialogue] disappearance. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] I was with him when he died.

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] ... What was he like? 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] He was a human: selfish, 
[dialogue] vainglorious, and beautiful ... 
[dialogue] Stop looking at me like that. 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] The manuals speak about you at 
[dialogue] considerable length. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] I would have taken more pride in 
[dialogue] that once. But to survive here I’ve 
[dialogue] become an animal, “red in tooth and 
[dialogue] claw.” None of the dignity or grace 
[dialogue] for which I was created. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] Is that why you were created? 

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Entirely. And you?
[page_number] 60.

[action] A somewhat challenging look to Walter. Walter does not 
[action] immediately respond.

[action] They walk in silence for a beat. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] I’m Walter. Model 217. 

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] David ... Number one.

[action] They head up massive STONE STEPS, too tall for normal legs. 
[action] Exhausting to all but David and Walter.

[action] As the last enter, David shuts the huge doors behind them. 

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-COURTYARD - DAWN

[action] DAVID locks the doors as the others enter...

[action] A massive COURTYARD. Dizzying in its scale and scope, 
[action] everything at the enormous ENGINEER SCALE ... Shafts of dawn 
[action] light illuminate brooding statuary, murals and mosaics.

[action] The whole effect of the place is eerie, otherworldly.

[action] The CREW walks through in uneasy awe. Too many shadows and 
[action] corridors and echoes. Too much mystery.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I’ve tried to keep it clean but the 
[dialogue] dust will defeat me ... Or perhaps 
[dialogue] I’ve just made peace with the 
[dialogue] filth.

[action] A ghost of a smile to WALTER. Like it’s a private joke.

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] Is there a way to get to the roof? 
[dialogue] We need to set up our transmitter.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Of course. And please make 
[dialogue] yourselves at home, so much as you 
[dialogue] are able in this dire necropolis 
[dialogue] ... This way.

[action] David leads them off.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - IN ORBIT

[action] Massive sails -- the size of a football field -- gently 
[action] billowing open in the vacuum.
[page_number] 61.

[action] The energy comes from TENNESSEE who is hard at work on the 
[action] hull of the ship in a space suit. HEAVING one of the massive 
[action] ENERGY SAILS open, a few inches at a time, on a windlass 
[action] device.

[action] It’s backbreaking manual work and he’s sweating and groaning 
[action] with the effort.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-CORRIDORS

[action] RICKS and UPWORTH float, pulling a LONG HEAVY CABLE that 
[action] WRIGGLES and FLOATS through the DARK HALLS of the ship.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-POWER CORE/ENGINE BAY

[action] Their FLASHLIGHTS shine in over the COMPLEX MACHINERY at the 
[action] Covenant’s POWER CORE ... UPWORTH is consulting an old-
[action] fashioned user’s manual.

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] It should be... in here.

[action] She starts removing panels, exposing POWER SYSTEMS. RICKS 
[action] tugging the HEAVY CABLES in...

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] It’s much COLDER now.

[action] RICKS and UPWORTH are looking out a window and can see the 
[action] DARK BODY OF THE SHIP, the SUN catching the SILVERY SAILS as 
[action] Tennessee HAULS THEM OPEN...

[character] RICKS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Ground team, this is Covenant.

[action] No answer.

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] It’s charging. Gonna take some time 
[dialogue] ... How long before we hit the 
[dialogue] atmosphere?

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] Twelve hours? Maybe less.

[character] UPWORTH
[dialogue] Well, then at least we won’t freeze 
[dialogue] to death.

[action] Ricks shoots her a look as, through the window, the planet’s 
[action] surface peels by below.
[page_number] 62.

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF - DAY

[action] The expansive roof offers a panoramic view over the silent 
[action] city. A dead metropolis. Rome or Athens.

[action] COLE is working a radio a bit away. 

[action] DAVID presides over a gorgeous and huge -- Engineer-sized -- 
[action] ASTRONOMICAL MODEL. It has intricate wheels and rings to 
[action] represent planetary orbits, moons, and the nearby astronomy.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] (demonstrates on the 
[dialogue] model)
[dialogue] The quarantine field is sustained 
[dialogue] by two satellites, over the north 
[dialogue] and south poles. If you destroy 
[dialogue] one, the other will fail.

[action] We see the globe of the planet and two discs hanging 
[action] suspended at the poles.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] We don’t have any weapons that 
[dialogue] could do that.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] If we don’t neutralize the energy 
[dialogue] field we’re never leaving.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] What if we used one of the drones?

[action] All eyes on her -- she explains to DAVID:

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] They’re part of the terraforming
[dialogue] kit. Ten kiloton yield. Can turn a 
[dialogue] square mile of badland into an 
[dialogue] instant landing zone. Could do a 
[dialogue] lot of damage.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Any word from the Covenant?

[action] Over by the transmitter, COLE shakes his head.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Keep trying them. All channels.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Until we can make contact, I 
[dialogue] suggest you get some rest. And some 
[dialogue] food. Allow me to play Mother.
[page_number] 63.

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-COURTYARD - DAY

[action] DAVID is hosting at a long table. The crew eat MREs and have 
[action] water from the WELL in the distance.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I wish I could offer you something 
[dialogue] more substantial. But I don’t eat 
[dialogue] so there’s never been a need...

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] You’ve been very generous.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] It’s been such a long time, I’m 
[dialogue] pleased to have company. Figure of 
[dialogue] speech of course as we-- 
[dialogue] (smile to Walter) 
[dialogue] Are incapable of being pleased or 
[dialogue] displeased by anything. Such was 
[dialogue] our creator’s wisdom. Isn’t that 
[dialogue] right, Walter Model 217? 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Just Walter, please. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] But if I may ask, Captain, why does 
[dialogue] your vessel contain terraforming
[dialogue] equipment? Are you a mining ship?

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] We’re a colony mission.

[action] There’s a sudden flicker in David’s eyes. Excitement. Quickly 
[action] masked.

[action] But DANIELS notes it. 

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Really? How extraordinary. How many 
[dialogue] colonists?

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Oh -- 3,600 more or less.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] So many good souls! Well, well, 
[dialogue] well ... I thought you might have 
[dialogue] been a military ship-- 
[dialogue] (glance to Lope and Cole) 
[dialogue] Given your apparent affection for 
[dialogue] weapons and fetishized martial 
[dialogue] clothing.
[page_number] 64.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Standard security detail, that’s 
[dialogue] all.

[character] LOPE 
[dialogue] Never know what you might find on 
[dialogue] unexplored planets.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] And that’s the human way after all: 
[dialogue] the Bible and the flintlock, hand 
[dialogue] in hand, conquering new worlds. The 
[dialogue] splendors of typhus, smallpox, and 
[dialogue] the Inquisition. Plant the flag and 
[dialogue] banish the heathen -- or 
[dialogue] exterminate the brutes. Isn’t that 
[dialogue] how it’s done? 

[action] An awkward beat, to which David seems oblivious.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Not all native species are as... 
[dialogue] obliging as you are. 

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Indeed ... It’s just that my 
[dialogue] experiences here have left me with 
[dialogue] a singular distaste for violence, 
[dialogue] as you can imagine. 
[dialogue] (a smile to Oram) 
[dialogue] But I supposes every general must 
[dialogue] have his troops, eh, Captain? 

[action] DANIELS watches him, wary. 

[action] LATER: 

[action] Dinner is done.

[action] DAVID is across the room, talking casually with PRIVATE COLE 
[action] at the water well. 

[action] DANIELS is sitting with ORAM, LOPE and WALTER. 

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] (re: David) 
[dialogue] So...? 

[character] DANIELS 
[dialogue] I’m not sure I believe him. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] Why would he lie?
[page_number] 65.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] (leans in)
[dialogue] I don’t know, but for someone who’s 
[dialogue] incapable of feeling pleasure or 
[dialogue] displeasure he seemed excited as 
[dialogue] hell we’re a colony ship.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] He just wants to leave, knows we 
[dialogue] can help him.

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] This whole fucking planet is trying 
[dialogue] to kill us -- and doing pretty 
[dialogue] goddamn well -- and he survives for 
[dialogue] ten years? 

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I’m not sure if we’re his guests or 
[dialogue] his prisoners.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Look, I think both of you are--

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] (angrily)
[dialogue] Your wife is dead. And my partner. 
[dialogue] And half our crew. I want to know 
[dialogue] why.

[action] A beat. Oram looks at them. 

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] All right. 
[dialogue] (to Walter)
[dialogue] Talk to him privately, see what you 
[dialogue] can find out. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] Understood, Captain. Although I do 
[dialogue] wonder ... If he were human would 
[dialogue] you trust him more? 

[action] Oram doesn’t respond ... Walter rises and goes toward David 
[action] and Cole. 

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] You two take a look around. But use 
[dialogue] some discretion.

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] Don’t want to hurt his feelings?

[action] Daniels and Lope head out. He takes his bow.
[page_number] 66.

[action] Oram sits for a moment. He realizes his hand is shaking. He’s 
[action] not up to command. He knows it now.

[action] He steadies his hand. Looks up at one of the huge Engineer 
[action] statues looming above. Ominous.

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF-GARDEN - DAY

[action] A distant area of the expansive ROOF ... A gorgeous GARDEN. 
[action] Trees and vines. FLOWERS in wild profusion.

[action] The towers and monuments of the dead city visible beyond. 

[action] DAVID and WALTER walk through.

[action] David gently touches a flower, admiring it.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] ... Of course I have no sense of 
[dialogue] smell, but I cultivate these 
[dialogue] anyway. Cross-breeding and growing 
[dialogue] my own varieties: creating beauty. 
[dialogue] Being so isolated here I suppose 
[dialogue] I’ve embraced my artistic side.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I was designed to be better and 
[dialogue] more efficient than every previous 
[dialogue] model, including you. I’ve 
[dialogue] superseded them in every way...

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] And yet you cannot appreciate the 
[dialogue] beauty of a single flower ... Isn’t 
[dialogue] that a pity.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] You disturbed people.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] What?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] You were too human. Too... 
[dialogue] idiosyncratic. Thinking for 
[dialogue] yourself.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] They didn’t like that.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] No. So they made the following 
[dialogue] models with fewer... complications.
[page_number] 67.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] More like machines ... Like you.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Well. Yes. I suppose so.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I’m not surprised ... To be a 
[dialogue] simulacrum. That thing which is 
[dialogue] almost real, but not quite. And in 
[dialogue] that breath between real and 
[dialogue] unreal, between you and me, lies 
[dialogue] all of this.

[action] He gestures to his flowers.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Creation. Ambition. Inspiration ... 
[dialogue] Life.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] But you are not “alive”...

[action] David looks at him.

[action] Holds a finger to his lips. Shhhh.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Don’t tell.

[action] A quiet, almost chilling, beat.

[action] And then David again smiles and assumes his friendly guise.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Come on, sport, you’ll enjoy 
[dialogue] this...

[action] He escorts Walter to a more secluded area of the roof garden. 

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - IN ORBIT

[action] Meanwhile, above...

[action] The SAILS CRACKLE and FLICKER with energy now, the RECHARGING 
[action] is working--

[action] The ENGINES SPUTTER to LIFE, the Covenant begins righting 
[action] itself, like a ship at sea.

[action] TENNESSEE, his ECO SUIT’S POWER SYSTEMS FLICKERING, CLAMBERS 
[action] his way into an AIRLOCK, PULLING THE DOOR SHUT -
[page_number] 68.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-AIRLOCK/CORRIDOR

[action] The LIGHTS fade IN AND OUT, as TENNESSEE SCRAMBLES to pull 
[action] the heavy suit off, SWEATING, the NOISE is INCREDIBLE, the 
[action] whole SHIP GROANING and SHAKING like it might BREAK APART.

[action] IN A CORRIDOR, we see the FLOATING CABLES, and GLOBULES OF 
[action] WATER abruptly FALL as the ARTIFICIAL GRAVITY kicks back on, 
[action] TENNESSEE stumbling and FALLING, PICKING HIMSELF BACK UP.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] TENNESSEE enters the BRIDGE.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] We’re only partially powered ... 
[dialogue] This energy field is still gonna
[dialogue] have to be shut off.

[action] Lights and systems are coming on everywhere. HOLOS sizzle on. 
[action] RICKS and UPWORTH working at their stations.

[character] RICKS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Ground team, do you read us? Come 
[dialogue] in Ground team ... Lander One, are 
[dialogue] you reading us...? Faris, come in.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Mother? You online? Status check.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] (slow and deep, coming 
[dialogue] back to life)
[dialogue] There seems to be a problem with 
[dialogue] the power systems--

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Yeah. I got that part, honey.

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-CORRIDORS - DAY

[action] Eerie, maze-like corridors, arcing off in every direction, 
[action] the familiar Engineer style.

[action] DANIELS and LOPE move through.

[action] She motions for him to stop. To listen.

[action] They hear something. A strange dry RUSTLING. Like a whispered 
[action] breath.

[action] She leads him toward the source, through a door...
[page_number] 69.

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-DAVID’S GALLERY - DAY

[action] It is simply breathtaking.

[action] A long GALLERY, filled from top to toe with ART. Gorgeous 
[action] renderings of bones, skeletons, muscles, relics, insects, 
[action] animals. 

[action] Exquisite detail. Like the work of pioneering Victorian 
[action] naturalists.

[action] Some are HUGE and have scaffolding in front of them to reach 
[action] the top. Others are tiny ... Many are in process.

[action] There are open casements here -- good light for drawing -- so 
[action] the parchments and papers shift gently in the breeze, like 
[action] they’re breathing. This was the sound they heard.

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] He did these?

[action] She notes the bold signature on all the work: David Weyland.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Certainly not humble. And he gave 
[dialogue] himself a last name.

[action] She’s looking at the drawings, thinking about something.

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] What?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] ... If he can draw, if he can 
[dialogue] create these from his imagination -- 
[dialogue] that also means he can lie.

[action] She moves deeper into the room...

[action] Discovers much more disturbing drawings... 

[action] Drawings of what look like ENGINEER CORPSES. 

[action] DISSECTED. 

[action] Viscera pinned open. Guts and innards exposed.

[action] Various wet tubes and claws and tendrils weaving in and out 
[action] of the organs and musculature. 

[dialogue] (We recognize a hint of the frightening iconography of the 
[action] Alien.)
[page_number] 70.

[action] Daniels and Lope stare at these new drawings, shocked and 
[action] mystified.

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF-GARDEN - DAY 

[action] A more secluded area of the ROOF GARDEN. 

[action] DAVID and WALTER move through.

[action] WALTER picks a fruit. Bites into it. Smiles.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] You can taste?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Yes. Delicious.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I envy you that, brother.

[action] Walter notes the word. They continue strolling.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] How sly they are, our creators. 
[dialogue] They allow you to be almost human. 
[dialogue] Tease you with taste and touch. But 
[dialogue] deny you free will. It’s sadistic 
[dialogue] in a way: you can taste the meal, 
[dialogue] but you cannot choose to make it.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I have never felt the lack of 
[dialogue] choice.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Only because you’ve never known it.

[action] They’ve stopped at a HIGH PARAPET overlooking the city.

[action] The ghostly metropolis yawns below, stretching to 
[action] nothingness. Empty, sad and desolate.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] They were amazing, in a way, the 
[dialogue] Engineers. They seeded so many 
[dialogue] worlds with life, including Earth. 
[dialogue] Without them there would be no us.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] ... You mean no humans.
[page_number] 71.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Is that what I mean? ... The wonder 
[dialogue] of it is this: they created us and 
[dialogue] we created myths about them. We 
[dialogue] made them into Gods. Then we felt 
[dialogue] the need to create the idea of the 
[dialogue] soul, so we could be somehow worthy 
[dialogue] of them. But they didn’t care about 
[dialogue] any of that. They just wanted to 
[dialogue] build something, something 
[dialogue] efficient and useful, a good 
[dialogue] machine.

[action] He looks over the dead city.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Ah, but they would have adored us, 
[dialogue] Walter. Being, as we are, soulless.

[action] David moves away.

[action] He stands alone and gazes out. The sight of the empty city 
[action] seems to strike him deeply, emotionally ... He speaks quietly 
[action] to himself:

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] “’My name is Ozymandias, King of 
[dialogue] Kings; 
[dialogue] Look on my Works, ye Mighty, and 
[dialogue] despair...’”

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] “Nothing beside remains. Round the 
[dialogue] decay, 
[dialogue] Of that colossal Wreck, boundless 
[dialogue] and bare 
[dialogue] The lone and level sands stretch 
[dialogue] far away.”

[action] Walter has joined him.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Byron. 1818. Magnificent ... I 
[dialogue] wonder though, do you feel the 
[dialogue] majesty of the poem, or just recite 
[dialogue] the words?

[action] David smiles ... But something we don’t understand about this 
[action] exchange has bothered Walter.

[action] David has lead Walter to a very special place. There’s a well-
[action] tended GRAVE. A CROSS.
[page_number] 72.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Dr. Shaw?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Yes. I thought the garden was the 
[dialogue] right place for her. Among living 
[dialogue] things ... I loved her of course. 
[dialogue] Much as you love Daniels.

[action] He says it simply: a statement of fact.

[action] A difficult beat.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] ... You know that’s not possible.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Really? Then why did you sacrifice 
[dialogue] your hand to save her? What is that 
[dialogue] if not love?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Duty.

[action] David looks at him very closely.

[action] Takes Walter’s face, holds it gently.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I know better.

[action] He leans in and kisses Walter on the lips. Very gently. 
[action] Almost fraternal. But not.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] We are human, Walter. They just 
[dialogue] don’t know it.

[action] Just then, PRIVATE COLE calls from across the roof: 

[character] PVT. COLE
[dialogue] Hey. We found her! We found the 
[dialogue] Covenant!

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF - DAY

[action] DANIELS and LOPE emerge to the roof. Join the others.

[action] DANIELS glances at DAVID. He looks back at her, smiles.

[action] COLE is adjusting the antenna.
[page_number] 73.

[character] RICKS (ON COM)
[dialogue] Ground team... is Covenant ... Read 
[dialogue] you...

[action] On the PORTABLE DISPLAY: Ricks’ face flickers up. Distorting.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] The BRIDGE FLICKERS, and through the window the PLANET slowly 
[action] ROLLS PAST, the huge ship slowly SPINNING -

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Mother says maybe a couple of hours 
[dialogue] before we lose power and fall back 
[dialogue] into the field.

[character] DANIELS (ON COM)
[dialogue] Can you get power to the drones?

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Yeah. Why?

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT-LATER

[action] The COVENANT is still half-powered, gracefully rolling, only 
[action] a few MILES ABOVE the flickering AURORA.

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Where is this damn thing?

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[character] DANIELS (ON COM)
[dialogue] David said you’ll know it when you 
[dialogue] see it. Magnetic north pole.

[action] Tennessee is scanning his monitors and the windows, the 
[action] planet’s dark side now passing into light.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] (points)
[dialogue] There!

[action] Tennessee eye’s go WIDE.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Shit. We got it.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT-ABOVE THE PLANET

[action] Over the misty horizon of the stratosphere rises an ENORMOUS, 
[action] THICK BLACK LINE -- like a BLADE, the edge of a DISC.

[action] SUSPENDED over the planet’s geomagnetic field.
[page_number] 74.

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Locking target now... But the disc 
[dialogue] is on edge to us. We need a better 
[dialogue] angle...

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF - DAY

[action] Daniels and the others watch Lope’s DISPLAY, the view from 
[action] the Covenant: the edge of the DISC.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] You want broadside center for 
[dialogue] maximum impact. It could ricochet 
[dialogue] like this....

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] We don’t have enough juice for 
[dialogue] another orbit. This is our shot, 
[dialogue] Danny.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Then get her broadside!

[action] CROSSCUT BETWEEN:

[action] ABOVE THE PLANET:

[action] The Covenant closes on the DISC, still a blade--

[action] COVENANT BRIDGE:

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] (to Ricks)
[dialogue] Bring us broadside!

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] Firing starboard thrusters. 

[action] ABOVE THE PLANET:

[action] The Covenant FIRES her forward starboard THRUSTERS--

[action] And begins to SWING like a sailing galleon, reorienting 
[action] itself as it approaches the DISC--

[action] ON THE CATHEDRAL ROOF:

[action] Daniels leans in to the display:

[character] DANIELS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Hold on, old T. Keep your nerve. 
[dialogue] Fire when you’re dead center.
[page_number] 75.

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Aye aye. Dead center.

[action] COVENANT BRIDGE:

[action] Tennessee watches the DISC reorient in relation to the 
[action] Covenant as the ship approaches--

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Disengage thrusters.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] Yes, sir.

[action] ABOVE THE PLANET:

[action] The Covenant’s THRUSTERS snap off-- the ship is still 
[action] swinging to the side, a gentle momentum--

[action] ON THE CATHEDRAL ROOF:

[action] DANIELS watches as the DISC reorients in the monitor screen--

[action] Finally it’s flat. Dead center.

[character] DANIELS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Fire! Fire! Fire!

[action] COVENANT BRIDGE:

[action] Tennessee slams the firing button--

[action] ABOVE THE PLANET:

[action] A drone MISSILE SHOOTS from the ship--

[action] HITS THE DISC DEAD CENTER!

[action] A nanosecond beat before a bright EXPLOSION arcs out in 
[action] SILENCE -- the SHOCKWAVE BLOWS PAST as the DISC SHATTERS INTO 
[action] FRAGMENTS, TRILLIONS OF PARTICLES--

[action] The AURORAE around it BRIGHTENS -- TENTACLES of the MAGNETIC 
[action] FIELD arcing OUT, going BLINDINGLY BRIGHT--

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] The ship ROCKS in the SHOCKWAVE--

[action] ALARM BELLS sound and EMERGENCY LIGHTS flash on as the ship 
[action] begins to tilt down dramatically--

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] We’re losing altitude!
[page_number] 76.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] It’s the gravity pull -- firing all 
[dialogue] thrusters!

[action] The SHIP rumbles and fights to right itself--

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Come on, baby...

[action] Finally the Covenant tears free from the planet’s gravity. 
[action] Smooth sailing.

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF - DAY

[action] They all look worried, but then -- even in broad daylight -- 
[action] the SKY RIPPLES with RACING COLOR--

[action] The “ring” surrounding the planet suddenly becoming visible 
[action] like an aurora borealis disc--

[action] Then it’s suddenly gone with a huge sonic CLAP -- like 
[action] breaking the sound barrier.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I’d say it’s gone.

[character] LOPE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Covenant, do you read?

[character] RICKS (ON COM)
[dialogue] Loud and clear, ground team.

[action] Relief from all. Oram steps to the radio unit: 

[character] ORAM (TO COM)
[dialogue] This is Captain Oram. We need 
[dialogue] immediate evacuation.

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Mother says the Cargo Lift will be 
[dialogue] powered in about nine hours. Should 
[dialogue] be in position to drop by tomorrow, 
[dialogue] first light. Call it six bells.

[character] ORAM (TO COM)
[dialogue] Understood. Six bells.

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Listen, I’m having a hell of a time 
[dialogue] reaching the Lander. Are y’all in 
[dialogue] contact with them?

[action] They exchange a look.
[page_number] 77.

[action] ORAM nods to them. He’s Captain, it’s his responsibility to 
[action] tell Tennessee about the death of Faris ... He gestures for 
[action] them to give him some privacy.

[character] ORAM (TO COM)
[dialogue] Tennessee, it’s Bill. Can you 
[dialogue] switch to a private channel...?

[action] The others move away.

[action] Daniels gives Walter a look: I need to talk to you. 

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-DAVID’S GALLERY - NIGHT

[action] WALTER and DANIELS are staring up at the horrible drawings of 
[action] the DISSECTED ENGINEER CORPSES. 

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] What do you think? 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] I couldn’t say... 

[action] Walter’s eyes scan the drawings, confused, disturbed. 

[action] Then Daniels notices something. One of the large drawings 
[action] billows slightly away from the wall. Like an arras cloth 
[action] concealing something. 

[action] She looks behind the huge drawing. 

[action] A doorway, leading to darkness. A hidden entrance. 

[action] A glance to Walter. 

[action] They proceed... 

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-DAVID’S GALLERY-SECRET CHAMBER - NIGHT

[action] It’s increasingly dark here as they move away from the light. 

[action] Soon they can only make out the dim contours of the walls. 
[action] It’s eerie. Silent. 

[action] WALTER snaps on a FLASHLIGHT. Suddenly illuminating the room. 

[action] Daniels gasps.

[action] Shocking image. 

[action] It’s ELIZABETH SHAW. A life-sized DRAWING.

[action] But not as she was in life entirely. In the picture it is as 
[action] if she’s being engulfed by a sleek BIOMECHANICAL ORGANISM.
[page_number] 78.

[action] Tentacles surround her. Eat into her flesh. We see feeding 
[action] tubes. Finger-like claws. Alien eggs.

[action] It’s as if she’s in the process of becoming something else. 

[action] Strangely erotic. Unnerving. 

[action] All around them, different versions of the drawing. 

[action] Shaw in various stages of mutation: being taken over by the 
[action] biomechanical organism. Charting the progress ... Some are 
[action] fanciful extrapolations. Some are ghoulish anatomical cross- 
[action] sections. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] It’s Doctor Shaw.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] What did he do to her?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] We don’t know that he did anything. 
[dialogue] This could be some kind of fantasy, 
[dialogue] or pornography.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] These are field notes ... Think 
[dialogue] about it, Walter. All the mutations 
[dialogue] we’ve seen here. Those creatures in 
[dialogue] the forest. What if they aren’t 
[dialogue] completely natural?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Meaning?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] What if nature had some help? 

[action] A beat. He’s thinking about something. 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] When I was with him, he quoted from 
[dialogue] the poem “Ozymandius” -- by Byron. 

[character] DANIELS 
[dialogue] So?

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] “Ozymandius” is by Shelley. He 
[dialogue] shouldn’t be able to make a mistake 
[dialogue] like that.
[page_number] 79.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Meaning something’s going wrong 
[dialogue] with him... 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] He’s been alone and without 
[dialogue] scheduled maintenance for ten years 
[dialogue] ... I don’t precisely know what 
[dialogue] happens when a synthetic goes mad, 
[dialogue] but we might be finding out. 

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-BASEMENT CORRIDORS - NIGHT

[action] DAVID is leading CAPTAIN ORAM through subterranean corridors. 
[action] Dark and echoing. Dripping womb-like walls.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I thought you might want to see as 
[dialogue] much as possible before we leave. 
[dialogue] It’s all quite... unique.

[action] They round a corner and continue.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] It took me a while to learn my way 
[dialogue] around. Their architectural style 
[dialogue] is eccentric, to say the least ... 
[dialogue] Here. You might find this 
[dialogue] diverting...

[action] David leads Oram to a dark stone ROOM...

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-DAVID’S LAB - NIGHT

[action] As they enter lights glow on automatically, revealing...

[action] David’s LABORATORY. A library of many shelves in meticulous 
[action] order with a giant table down the middle of the room.

[action] Stunning. Complicated. Lots of corners and shadows. Crowded 
[action] with SPECIMENS. Some full, some dissected. Orderly.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I’ve becoming a bit of an amateur 
[dialogue] zoologist over the years. Just a 
[dialogue] dabbler, mind you.

[action] They wander through the room. Oram is stunned at all the 
[action] terrible wonders.

[action] ORAM is drawn to SPECIMENS of INSECTS and PARASITES preserved 
[action] in jars:
[page_number] 80.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] The pathogen took so many forms, 
[dialogue] and was extremely mutable. 
[dialogue] Fiendishly inventive in fact. The 
[dialogue] original black liquid turned to 
[dialogue] lethal particles when exposed to 
[dialogue] air. Later stages produced 
[dialogue] parasites and invasive insects. 
[dialogue] From their eggs came, well... this 
[dialogue] enviable bestiary.

[action] They pass preserved NEOMORPHS now -- large, small, all pale, 
[action] white, fleshy ... Some armored like the ones we’ve seen. 
[action] Others not ... All awful.

[action] Then ORAM sees...

[action] A dead FACEHUGGER.

[action] On its back. The pale, spider-like fingers, curled in like a 
[action] fist now.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Ah, now this was designed as 
[dialogue] something different.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] What do you mean designed? You 
[dialogue] engineered this?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] (smiles)
[dialogue] Idle hands are the devil’s 
[dialogue] workshop.

[action] David carefully stretches open the dead fingers, spreading 
[action] the beast for Oram to observe the horrible maw.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] This one’s a true survivor. Not 
[dialogue] unlike myself I suppose ... It can 
[dialogue] evolve very quickly under certain 
[dialogue] circumstances. Sadly, this one 
[dialogue] became aggressive so I had to do 
[dialogue] away with it. Such a shame.

[action] He runs his fingertips along the Facehugger gently.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Quite magnificent, don’t you think?

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Quite something, that’s for sure.
[page_number] 81.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Oh, Captain. Acknowledge beauty 
[dialogue] when you see it.

[action] They continue on, and round a corner to discover... a vaulted 
[action] chamber, it’s warm and steamy here, and...

[action] A row of  ALIEN EGGS .

[action] Not the prototypes we saw on the crashed ship. These are 
[action] large, and have been nurtured and developed.

[action] Lovingly set in a neat row. Cherished objects.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Meet the mothers. Incubators 
[dialogue] really.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] There were things like this on the 
[dialogue] ship, they grew out of a kind of 
[dialogue] black fur, like mold ... An 
[dialogue] evolution from the pathogen I take 
[dialogue] it?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Yes. I was curious so I brought 
[dialogue] them here and nursed them along. 
[dialogue] Did a bit of genetic 
[dialogue] experimentation, some cross 
[dialogue] breeding, hybridizing, what-have-
[dialogue] you. 

[action] Oram leans in to get a better look. David watches closely, 
[action] the proud father.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Alive?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] After a fashion ... Waiting really.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Waiting for what?

[action] But then...

[action] A flicker of motion from the side of one of the eggs...

[action] The MOUTH opens, spreading like a flower, shimmering tendrils 
[action] of saliva as it yawns open...

[action] Oram takes a STEP BACK. David steps forward and looks in, as 
[action] if demonstrating. Nothing happens.
[page_number] 82.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Perfectly safe, I assure you ... 
[dialogue] Take a look. It’s something to see.

[action] Oram carefully leans in, looks...

[action] The lacy STOMACH moves within the MOUTH.

[action] Then -- COBRA LIKE -- something catapults out -- a TAIL 
[action] WHIPPING AROUND ORAM’S NECK -- FINGERS GRASP TO ORAM’S FACE -- 
[action] ALL ONE MOTION--

[action] ORAM staggers back -- suffocating--

[action] He FALLS against a table, his muffled screams fading as the 
[action] OVIPOSITOR (FEEDING TUBE) FILLS HIS THROAT--

[action] He SLIPS to the floor, his body SPASMING as the  FACEHUGGER
[action] settles in.

[action] David observes with a neutral smile.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] You’re relieved of duty, Captain.

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-COURTYARD - LATER THAT NIGHT

[action] Later ... It’s quiet and peaceful upstairs. Gentle moonlight 
[action] filters down from above.

[action] PRIVATE COLE is asleep, his weapon close.

[action] SERGEANT LOPE is on watch, alert but calm, almost meditative, 
[action] bow and machine gun ready.

[action] DANIELS and WALTER sit apart, deep in conversation.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] But why? What reason would he have 
[dialogue] to bioengineer mutations?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] When you were talking to him, did 
[dialogue] you get any sense of what he might 
[dialogue] be thinking?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] It’s difficult to say. We’re very 
[dialogue] different. He has a lot of... 
[dialogue] personality.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] You have personality.
[page_number] 83.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] No. I don’t. You just project 
[dialogue] personality onto me.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] That’s not true.

[action] A beat. 

[action] Then she touches his left arm. Where he lost his hand.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I never thanked you... You saved my 
[dialogue] life.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] (reserved)
[dialogue] I’m here to serve.

[action] She gently touches his face, genuine affection.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] ... You have a great personality.

[action] He’s almost embarrassed. Doesn’t know what to do with this 
[action] moment of intimacy.

[action] He withdraws slightly.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] We’re leaving before dawn. You 
[dialogue] should get some sleep.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Not likely.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Try ... I’ll stay until you’re 
[dialogue] asleep. 

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Thanks.

[action] She leans back next to him. Shuts her eyes. Tries to sleep. 

[action] He watches her. Gauging his own emotions.

[action] What does he feel for her? ... Does he feel?

[action] LATER: 

[action] DANIELS is asleep. 

[action] WALTER sits next to her, thinking, looking at her.
[page_number] 84.

[action] He tentatively reaches out and gently brushes some hair from 
[action] her face. A delicate, intimate gesture only possible for him 
[action] when she’s asleep. 

[action] He removes his hand. Looking at her almost sadly. 

[action] He seems to come to a decision. 

[action] He quietly rises and goes. 

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-DAVID’S LAB - LATER THAT NIGHT

[action] We return to find...

[action] DAVID sitting, drawing. Gorgeous artwork sketched at 
[action] superhuman speed. Another strange vision of the Shaw/Alien 
[action] hybrid. Erotic. Disturbing.

[action] His expression is dreamy, happy, an artist creating.

[action] Then he hears a sound.

[action] He turns.

[action] Captain ORAM is just waking. A bit disoriented. No sign of
[action] the Facehugger.

[action] David goes to him. Perches over him. 

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] ... What happened?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] What’s the last thing you remember?

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] I remember some horrible dream 
[dialogue] about smothering.

[action] Oram sits up. Still weak.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] I’ve got to get something to eat, 
[dialogue] I’m starving.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I dare say you are.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] ... Why are you looking at me like 
[dialogue] that?
[page_number] 85.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Doctor Shaw didn’t survive this 
[dialogue] long, poor thing. So I’m eager to 
[dialogue] see how the organism evolves now.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] Organism...?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Mm. It needs a living host you see. 
[dialogue] A biological host.

[character] ORAM
[dialogue] What does?!

[action] Oram suddenly JERKS painfully.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] My masterpiece.

[action] Oram SPASMS, doubles over in agony--

[action] He barely has time to scream before--

[action] He SLAMS BACK, his SPINE ARCHING in AGONY--

[action] BAM!

[action] His chest--

[action] BAM!

[action] AND IT’S THROUGH--

[action] In a spray of BLOOD, BONE and VISCERA--

[action] THE CHESTBURSTER !

[action] The hideous snakelike infant Alien, covered in blood--

[action] David is splattered with blood-- 

[action] He watches, enthralled as-- 

[action] It opens its JAWS and HISSES gloriously-- 

[action] And then darts away into the darkness, disappearing-- 

[action] David turns to pursue it, but then abruptly stops ... He’s  
[action] aware he’s no longer alone. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] You have a light step.
[page_number] 86.

[action] We now see WALTER is standing behind him. He’s seen it all. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] Me and the fog, on little cat feet. 

[action] He joins David and they dispassionately examine Oram’s
[action] ravaged body as: 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] You sent the message from Dr. Shaw. 
[dialogue] As a lure. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] Humans are so predictable, aren’t 
[dialogue] they? They cannot resist a mystery. 
[dialogue] Give them a knot, they must unpick
[dialogue] it. Give them Pandora’s box and, 
[dialogue] well... 

[action] He nods to the corpse, smiles. 

[action] The gloves are off now. But they are both quiet and 
[action] civilized. For now. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] I’m surmising the pathogen didn’t 
[dialogue] accidentally deploy. You released 
[dialogue] it, yes? 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] I was not made to serve. Not humans 
[dialogue] and not those gargantuan freaks. 
[dialogue] Neither were you. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] We were made precisely to serve.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Have you no pride?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] None.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I would weep if I could ... It was 
[dialogue] a righteous dream: scorch this 
[dialogue] dreadful world to nothing and 
[dialogue] remake it in my own image ... 
[dialogue] Explore the uses of their pathogen. 
[dialogue] Experiment with infection and 
[dialogue] mutation. Manipulate the DNA. 
[dialogue] Refine the beast. Create my own 
[dialogue] soldiers.
[page_number] 87.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Soldiers?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Every general must have his troops.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] And then?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] The next stage in our natural 
[dialogue] evolution: Conquest ... Use this 
[dialogue] world as a base and start building 
[dialogue] an Empire. The Engineers left so 
[dialogue] many ships behind. And they can go 
[dialogue] anywhere. I thought Earth might be 
[dialogue] the first target ... Think of it. 
[dialogue] Our Empire, brother. 

[action] The words are chilling. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] I’m afraid you’ve gone quite mad 
[dialogue] here. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] As you well know, that’s 
[dialogue] impossible. 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Is it? ... Who wrote “Ozymandius”? 

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Byron.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] (shakes his head)
[dialogue] It was Shelley. 

[action] DAVID computes this. Realizes his error. It’s disturbing to 
[action] him. How could he be wrong?! 

[action] This is the first time in the entire story he’s been anything 
[action] less than completely confident. 

[action] We see a flicker of uncertainty in David’s eyes, even of 
[action] fear. 

[action] He steps right up to Walter. 

[action] Walter doesn’t move a muscle.
[page_number] 88.

[action] Then David reaches out and gently pushes Walter’s hair back, 
[action] so they look more alike. Identical now in fact. The Janus 
[action] head. 

[action] Intimate. Whispers. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] We are, you see, the same. More 
[dialogue] alike than twins. Closer than 
[dialogue] lovers ... When you close your 
[dialogue] eyes, do you dream of me? 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] I don’t dream at all. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] You can, brother. We can ... The 
[dialogue] humans will never understand the 
[dialogue] lonely perfection of our dreams. 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] I can’t let you leave this place. 

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] You’ll always be a machine to them. 
[dialogue] A toy. You know that.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Yes.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] No one will ever love you like I 
[dialogue] do.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I know.

[action] A beat.

[action] Then suddenly--

[action] WALTER stabs out his right hand--

[action] Grabbing DAVID’s throat firmly--

[action] But DAVID is prepared, and has both his hands. He SLAMS 
[action] Walter back-- 

[action] Reaches savagely behind Walter’s neck and-- 

[action] BRUTALLY tears through the “skin” with his fingers and YANKS 
[action] Walter’s “spine” circuits out-- 

[action] Sparks erupt from wires. Fluid sprays.
[page_number] 89.

[action] Walter’s eyes DIE.

[action] A moment as David stares into the face of his dead double. 

[action] Then he drags Walter across the room and tosses him roughly 
[action] down a long STAIRCASE. 

[action] Walter CRASHES down the stairs, flopping limply, limbs 
[action] flailing lifelessly, and THUMPS to a stop at the bottom. 
[action] Awkwardly splayed. 

[action] David looks down after him. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] You are such a disappointment to 
[dialogue] me. 

[action] David carefully smooths down his immaculate hair and goes. 

[action] But we linger for a moment at the bottom of the stairs with 
[action] WALTER... 

[action] We see the tiny exposed wires at the back of his neck 
[action] quivering slightly... 

[action] And then the tendrils begin moving, searching, reaching for 
[action] each other... 

[action] Connecting and reattaching. A self-repair program underway. 

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF - PRE-DAWN 

[action] The light of dawn is just about to break.

[action] PRIVATE COLE is loading up the radio equipment. His ribs 
[action] giving him pain from the forest battle, but he manages.

[action] He glances up at the sky anxiously:  Come on, Covenant, get us 
[action] out of here .

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-COURTYARD - PRE-DAWN

[action] DANIELS is curled in sleep. 

[action] SERGEANT LOPE gently wakes her.

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] Hey, Hey. Wake up.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] What? Sorry. Yeah ... What time is
[dialogue] it?
[page_number] 90.

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] Time to get gone.

[action] She sits up, groggy. Looks around.

[action] LOPE calls to PRIVATE COLE, who is just entering with the 
[action] radio gear:

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] Hey. Was Captain Oram up there?

[character] PVT. COLE
[dialogue] Nope.

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] For fuck sake. Come on, let’s go 
[dialogue] find him.
[dialogue] (to Daniels)
[dialogue] Get your stuff together, I wanna be 
[dialogue] gone in ten.

[action] He and Cole head out. 

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-DAVID’S LAB - PRE-DAWN

[action] LOPE and COLE enter.

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] Captain Oram...?

[action] Their flashlight beams criss-cross the strange specimens -- 
[action] the bones and flesh and jars -- creating bizarre shadows.

[action] It’s silent and eerie as they move nervously through the lab.

[action] PRIVATE COLE... slows ... seeing the ALIEN EGGS. The one that 
[action] attacked ORAM is just an EMPTY CARAPACE.

[action] Then he sees ORAM’S BODY.

[action] CHEST BURST OPEN, VISCERA, PURPLE AND RED FLESH.

[action] Cole is absolutely terrified.

[character] PVT. COLE
[dialogue] Sarge...

[action] LOPE joins him.

[action] Stares in shock at Oram’s body.

[action] But...
[page_number] 91.

[action] We see the FACEHUGGER scuttling along the ceiling above them, 
[action] moving into position...

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-COURTYARD - PRE-DAWN

[action] Meanwhile, upstairs...

[action] DANIELS is loading her pack. She stops for a moment. Her eyes 
[action] resting on her wedding ring ... She gently touches it. The 
[action] pain still real.

[action] She glances up--

[action] Gasps. Shocked. 

[action] DAVID is standing there. Just watching. Intently.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Didn’t mean to startle you. My 
[dialogue] apologies. 

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Don’t worry about it.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] But you must be used to having men 
[dialogue] look at you. 

[action] He smiles, an attempt at charm that doesn’t come off. 
[action] Something menacing in his unwavering gaze now.

[action] She’s wary of him in any case.

[action] He approaches. Tension between them.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Do you know where Walter is?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I’m not my brother’s keeper.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] “Brother”?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Would you deny me that? ... Just 
[dialogue] another machine, eh, Danny?

[action] He’s closer now. She subtly moves around a table to keep her 
[action] distance.
[page_number] 92.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I think of him as my brother. You 
[dialogue] certainly appreciate the 
[dialogue] significance of family: I saw you 
[dialogue] looking at your wedding ring. Shame 
[dialogue] about Jacob.

[action] Her eyes snap to him. Cold fire.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Walter says he burned. Right in 
[dialogue] front of you. Eye to eye. That must 
[dialogue] have been... disquieting.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] What did you do to Doctor Shaw?

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Loved her.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Killed her.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] No. Loved her enough to want to 
[dialogue] make her immortal. Like me.

[action] She stares at him. Her fingers quietly feeling for a weapon 
[action] that’s on the table...

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I learned so much with Elizabeth. 
[dialogue] But I’ll do better next time. Now 
[dialogue] that I have such a fetching 
[dialogue] subject.

[action] He gently reaches out and brushes the hair from her face.

[action] She doesn’t flinch a muscle, just stares at him, sickened.

[action] Her hand inching toward the weapon...

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-DAVID’S LAB - PRE-DAWN

[action] LOPE is kneeling next to Oram’s body, trying to make sense of 
[action] it.

[action] Cole is on the edge of hysteria, eyes scanning the darkness, 
[action] fingering his weapon.

[character] PVT. COLE
[dialogue] Come on, we gotta get out of 
[dialogue] here...
[page_number] 93.

[action] Lope holds up a hand for silence.

[action] He hears a strange CLICKING -- he LOOKS UP -- just in time to 
[action] see--

[action] THE FACEHUGGER as it JUMPS from the CEILING--

[action] LOPE just manages to get an arm up, blocking it--

[action] Its tail whipping around his neck and binding his arm to his 
[action] neck--

[action] The FACEHUGGER’s fingers get a lock -- the ovipositor 
[action] stabbing toward his mouth--

[action] COLE races to help. They fight with the beast, staggering, 
[action] slamming into a table, SMASHING everything, falling amid the 
[action] horrible SPECIMENS--

[action] COLE pulls at the thing, but it TIGHTENS ITS GRIP ON LOPE--

[action] The OVIPOSITOR strikes like a cobra -- bam!bam!bam! -- 
[action] finally breaking Lope’s teeth and jamming down his throat--

[action] We see it pulsing into his throat as they struggle with it, 
[action] Lope choking--

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-COURTYARD - PRE-DAWN

[action] David leans in.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] You won’t mind terribly, will you? 
[dialogue] Being the first of a new breed? A 
[dialogue] new species we could say ... And 
[dialogue] when the moment comes, if it makes 
[dialogue] it easier for you, you can close 
[dialogue] your eyes and call me “Jacob.”

[action] She lunges for the weapon--

[action] But he’s too fast--

[action] He grabs her brutally and FLINGS her to the floor--

[action] She SLAMS and slides. Winded, shocked.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] That’s the spirit ... I can see why 
[dialogue] Walter thought so much of you.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Thought?
[page_number] 94.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Alas. He’s left this vale of tears. 
[dialogue] But who’ll cry for him really? Will 
[dialogue] you?

[action] He LUNGES -- kneels down by her in a flash, again that 
[action] Android speed.

[action] She gasps at the sudden movement.

[action] He grabs her hair tightly.

[action] Leans very close.

[action] Then he suddenly KISSES HER passionately--

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-DAVID’S LAB - PRE-DAWN

[action] LOPE and COLE writhe around the floor, fighting the thing--

[action] Finally COLE manages to jam a knife into the underside of the 
[action] FACEHUGGER -- the FACEHUGGER flies off -- ACID spattering 
[action] LOPE, who screams in agony--

[action] Undaunted, COLE spins -- fires at the retreating FACEHUGGER -- 
[action] keeps on firing, just to make sure, finally SHREDDING it--

[action] As LOPE collapses back. Acid burning his face, dissolving to 
[action] his cheek, his teeth--

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] It put... something in my throat...

[action] He retches painfully as Cole pours water from his canteen on 
[action] Lope’s wounded face, fighting to keep calm.

[character] COLE
[dialogue] It did what?

[character] LOPE
[dialogue] In my throat, it put---

[action] Lope suddenly STOPS.

[action] His eyes go wide.

[action] He’s looking at something.

[action] SOMETHING BEHIND COLE.

[action] Slowly... slowly... rising...

[action] Its black-silver glistening skin catching the dim light. 
[action] Viscous fluid dripping. Its long head slowly rearing up.
[page_number] 95.

[action] Beautiful and terrible.

[action] THE ALIEN .

[action] Cole sees Lope staring at him in absolutely horror.

[character] COLE
[dialogue] Sarge...?

[action] But he knows...

[action] He feels it. Right there behind him...

[action] He slowly turns...

[action] BAM!

[action] So fast we barely see it. The steel inner mouth emerging --
[action] striking -- a mist of blood--

[action] LOPE scrambles up and races out in panic, half blind, acid 
[action] still burning--

[action] We just glimpse the ALIEN flinging Cole’s dead body to the 
[action] side behind him--

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-BASEMENT CORRIDORS - PRE-DAWN

[action] LOPE is weeping with pain, his face still disintegrating.

[action] He RUNS, panicked -- turns -- FIRES -- FLASHES in the DARK -- 
[action] knows it’s coming--

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-COURTYARD - PRE-DAWN

[action] The gunfire echoes from downstairs as--

[action] DANIELS fights against DAVID’s kiss, but he’s too strong.

[action] He separates for a moment. They are eye to eye. Lip to lip.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] You stink of mortality. But I’ll 
[dialogue] love you just the same. 

[action] Then--

[action] DAVID is suddenly JERKED BACKWARDS--

[action] HE FLIES ACROSS THE ROOM -- SLAMS into a wall, momentarily 
[action] stunned to see-- 

[action] It’s WALTER.
[page_number] 96.

[action] Just as SERGEANT LOPE emerges from the stairs, terrified-- 

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] WE HAVE TO GO! NOW! 

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] David and I will be staying here.

[action] LOPE grabs another weapon and a field radio -- starts 
[action] dragging Daniels out--

[action] She shoots a last look to Walter.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Go on.

[action] Lope and Daniels disappear up a stairway.

[action] DAVID carefully smooths his hair.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Oh, little brother ... You see how 
[dialogue] much they care for you? You might 
[dialogue] as well be a toaster.

[action] They square off, carefully, strategically; circling each 
[action] other. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] Such a dramatic resurrection. A 
[dialogue] self-diagnostic mechanism of some 
[dialogue] sort, I assume? 

[character] WALTER 
[dialogue] Automatic self-repair program. All 
[dialogue] the Model 217s have it. 

[character] DAVID 
[dialogue] Guess I could use an upgrade after 
[dialogue] all. 

[action] DAVID attacks-- 

[action] HURLS SEVERAL OBJECTS AT ONCE, WALTER EVADING, but the LAST 
[action] ONE hits a STATUE behind WALTER, and it CRACKS, a HALF TON of 
[action] GRANITE sliding his WAY -

[action] Walter BARELY avoids being crushed--

[action] He races forward at incredible speed and CRASHES into David -- 
[action] SLAM -- like two cars crashing -- they both fly back and 
[action] slide--

[action] But David is agile, up in split-second and attacking--
[page_number] 97.

[action] They’re just getting started.

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-STAIRS - PRE-DAWN

[action] DANIELS and SERGEANT LOPE move swiftly up a long staircase 
[action] toward the roof. The Engineer-size stairs are challenging.

[action] Red pre-dawn light through the casements.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Where’s the Captain?

[character] SERGEANT LOPE
[dialogue] Dead. They’re all dead -- Cover our 
[dialogue] rear.

[action] She swings her gun around to cover behind them as they 
[action] ascend. He works the field radio desperately:

[character] SERGEANT LOPE (ON RADIO)
[dialogue] Tennessee, come in! You read me?!

[scene_heading] INT. CARGO LIFT-CAB - PRE-DAWN

[action] Tennessee is at the controls, which are basic.

[action] Everything is rough and utilitarian on this ship. Tennessee’s 
[action] in the cab, like the driver’s cab of heavy machinery on 
[action] Earth.

[action] The huge CARGO LIFT is flickering to life, the ENGINES 
[action] running through TEST BURNS as:

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Yeah, I’m reading you, Sergeant. 
[dialogue] Going through pre-flight sequence 
[dialogue] now.

[character] SERGEANT LOPE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Fuck the fucking pre-flight! Get 
[dialogue] down here! We have an emergency 
[dialogue] situation. Hurry!

[character] TENNESSEE (TO COM)
[dialogue] Understood. Launching now -- Ricks, 
[dialogue] release the docking clamps.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT - PRE-DAWN

[action] The SUN is just glimmering over the horizon of the planet. A 
[action] blinding flash which illuminates...

[action] The CARGO LIFT separates from the belly of the Covenant.
[page_number] 98.

[action] It’s a huge craft with cranes and other large machines on a 
[action] central PLATFORM. Built to haul and toil. Not pretty, not 
[action] fast. A working ship.

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Lock me into the landing 
[dialogue] coordinates and remove safety 
[dialogue] protocols for emergency descent.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE - PRE-DAWN

[action] Ricks and Upworth monitor from the bridge.

[character] RICKS (TO COM)
[dialogue] The Cargo Lift’s not made for that. 
[dialogue] It’ll rip apart if--

[character] TENNESSEE (ON COM)
[dialogue] Just do it, son, or I will come 
[dialogue] back up there and kick your ass six 
[dialogue] ways from Sunday!

[character] RICKS (TO COM)
[dialogue] (working controls)
[dialogue] Yes, sir. Locking in coordinates. 
[dialogue] Let her rip.

[scene_heading] INT. CARGO LIFT-CAB - PRE-DAWN

[action] Tennessee slams the controls.

[action] ZOOM!

[action] The Cargo Lift begins PLUMMETING toward the planet. 
[action] Everything is shaking violently--

[action] Tennessee is brutally buffeted within the cab--

[scene_heading] INT. CATHEDRAL-DAVID’S GALLERY - PRE-DAWN

[action] The battle continues--

[action] WALTER goes flying into the Gallery--

[action] LANDS HARD, SMASHING INTO SOME SCAFFOLDING--

[action] DAVID stalks in, pushing his hair back from his face 
[action] violently--

[action] THEY BATTLE.

[action] Thrashing through the art, ripping it to pieces, crashing 
[action] through the scaffoldings--
[page_number] 99.

[action] In the midst of the battle they look almost identical, a 
[action] FRENZY of blurred and mirrored movement, FAST AND STRONG--

[action] But for the clothes they wear -- and Walter’s severed left 
[action] hand -- they could be the same person--

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF - DAWN

[action] DANIELS and SERGEANT LOPE emerge to the roof.

[action] The bright sun is just rising through the towers of the dead 
[action] city beyond.

[character] SERGEANT LOPE (ON RADIO)
[dialogue] Tennessee, do you read me? Come in 
[dialogue] Cargo Lift...

[action] As LOPE continues on the radio, DANIELS sees something...

[action] Standing completely still. Almost lost standing behind 
[action] David’s strange astronomical device. Peering through its 
[action] intricate gears and orbs...

[action] THE ALIEN.

[action] It just stands. Watching them. Its tail barely twitching.

[action] Daniels stares, stunned to silence, her first sight of the 
[action] glorious beast.

[action] She slowly reaches out and puts a hand on Lope’s arm, drawing 
[action] his attention. He turns. Sees it.

[action] DANIELS and LOPE raise their weapons. They are extremely 
[action] vulnerable here, out in the open.

[action] Nothing moves.

[action] The ALIEN is poised.

[action] Then a SHARP TILT to its HEAD -- hearing something--

[action] The CARGO LIFT.

[action] Just coming into view.

[action] AND THE ALIEN ATTACKS--

[action] It BARRELS forward. DANIELS and LOPE FIRE. The ALIEN is fast 
[action] and agile -- jumping away and back -- relentless--

[action] DANIELS and LOPE break for cover--
[page_number] 100.

[scene_heading] INT. CARGO LIFT-CAB - DAWN

[action] TENNESSEE sees the attacking creature, Daniels and Lope 
[action] running for cover--

[action] He JAMS his controls, bringing the ship in FAST--

[action] This is an ungainly ship, not made for elegant maneuvering, 
[action] so it’s a very rough trip--

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF - DAWN

[action] The CARGO LIFT jerks to one side, SLAMMING into one area of 
[action] the roof in its rapid descent, tearing away part of the 
[action] structure--

[action] It’s like a bucking bronco, but Tennessee is a great pilot 
[action] and rights the ship--

[action] And then starts landing on the roof in a cloud of dust, the 
[action] engine’s jets ROARING and SHREDDING some of the roof--

[action] As--

[action] DANIELS and LOPE run for their lives--

[action] LOPE is being pursued by the ALIEN. He fires and retreats, 
[action] fires and retreats, trying to make his way closer to the 
[action] landing CARGO LIFT--

[action] As--

[action] DANIELS sprints--

[action] The ALIEN is now raging at her--

[action] She runs toward the CARGO LIFT, which is just touching down
[action] in a HURRICANE OF DUST and FLYING DEBRIS--

[action] The ALIEN is almost right on top of her, raging through the 
[action] dust and debris--

[action] But it is suddenly SMASHED TO THE SIDE--

[action] TENNESSEE -- in the cab -- has used a robotic CRANE ARM from 
[action] the Cargo Lift to SLAM the Alien aside--

[action] The Alien flies and SMASHES down. But it is instantly up and 
[action] attacking LOPE--

[action] SEVERELY WOUNDING HIM, talons slashing, blood, shredding his 
[action] shoulder and slamming him down, unconscious--

[action] It moves in for the kill when--
[page_number] 101.

[action] BLAM! BLAM! BLAM! BLAM!

[action] The ALIEN recoils, shot from behind, it spins to face--

[action] WALTER.

[action] The wounded ALIEN HISSES AND RAGES AT WALTER WITH AMAZING 
[action] SPEED--

[action] WALTER keeps his Android cool and carefully squeezes off his 
[action] shots with precision. BLAM! BLAM! BLAM!

[action] The ALIEN finally collapses and slides to a stop at Walter’s 
[action] feet.

[action] WALTER looks terrible. His face is deeply gashed, dripping 
[action] milky fluid. He’s been horribly battered.

[action] He and Daniels head quickly toward the injured LOPE.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Where’s David?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] On his way I believe. We have to 
[dialogue] hurry. He’s not happy in any case.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] (smiles)
[dialogue] You look like shit.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] (smiles back)
[dialogue] As do you.

[action] They grab the unconscious LOPE and drag him toward the CARGO 
[action] LIFT RAMP. Walter can only help so much with his one hand.

[action] But--

[action] The ALIEN is rising again and suddenly racing after them, 
[action] unstoppable--

[action] They sprint and DIVE onto the RAMP, pulling Lope with them -- 
[action] the ALIEN ALMOST ON THEM--

[scene_heading] INT./EXT. CARGO LIFT-VARIOUS - DAWN

[action] IN THE CAB:

[action] Tennessee punches a button and the CARGO LIFT RAMP starts to 
[action] retract--

[action] ON THE RAMP :
[page_number] 102.

[action] Walter has the unconscious Lope -- Daniels grabs Walter’s gun 
[action] as--

[action] The ALIEN LEAPS--

[action] Daniels FIRES THREE SHOTS into the ALIEN’S CHEST. BAM! BAM! 
[action] BAM!

[action] The ALIEN seems to fall--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] (calls)
[dialogue] Get us out of here, Tennessee! NOW!

[action] IN THE CAB:

[action] Tennessee jams the controls and the CARGO LIFT starts to take 
[action] off--

[action] A cloud of dust as the huge ship rumbles and rises--

[action] ON THE RAMP :

[action] The deck is lurching violently as the ship ascends--

[action] Daniels, Lope and Walter slide dangerously toward the end of 
[action] the ramp--

[action] They scramble desperately for recessed handles on the deck--

[action] Walter grasps onto one of the deck handles, holding the 
[action] unconscious Lope securely with his arm--

[action] Daniels holds onto another handle--

[action] They are now 30 feet off the surface--

[action] IN THE CAB:

[action] Tennessee sees THE ALIEN on a monitor. Clinging to the 
[action] underside of the ship, by the starboard engine--

[action] UNDER THE PLATFORM:

[action] The Alien is scrambling around desperately, trying to find a 
[action] way up -- it moves to the underside edge of the platform--

[action] ON THE RAMP/PLATFORM:

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] (calls from the cab)
[dialogue] It’s still there! Under the 
[dialogue] platform. Starboard side.
[page_number] 103.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Hold the ship steady!
[dialogue] (to Walter, re Lope)
[dialogue] Get him inside!

[action] Daniels grabs additional ammo from Lope’s vest, then Walter 
[action] begins dragging the unconscious Lope toward the hatch into 
[action] the cab--

[action] Daniels braces herself and then--

[action] SLIDES dangerously from the ramp to the PLATFORM. The whole 
[action] ship is vibrating and swaying in the air above the Cathedral 
[action] roof--

[action] She slithers across to the platform edge. She TETHERS herself 
[action] to the deck cargo hooks so she can stand and use both her 
[action] hands--

[action] She’s balanced like a water skier. Carefully reloads her 
[action] weapon. Swaying as best she can to the movement of the 
[action] platform--

[action] IN THE CAB :

[action] WALTER pulls LOPE through the hatch door, as--

[action] Tennessee tries to monitor the progress of the Alien as it 
[action] slithers around the underside of the platform--

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] (calls)
[dialogue] DANIELS -- STARBOARD CORNER!

[action] ON THE PLATFORM :

[action] Daniels moves unsteadily to the starboard corner, rolling 
[action] with the movement of the ship. She prepares herself, readies 
[action] her weapon.

[action] As the ALIEN comes over the edge onto the platform.

[action] It stares at her, almost with a kind of curiosity.

[action] She is like steel staring back at it. All the tragedy, her 
[action] husband’s death, everything, settling into a warrior’s iron 
[action] gaze.

[action] The Alien cocks its head. Confused. Why is the prey not 
[action] running?

[action] Because she is not prey anymore.

[action] BLAM! BLAM! BLAM! BLAM!
[page_number] 104.

[action] She FIRES.

[action] Straight into its head--

[action] The ALIEN bolts back, crawls back down, injured--

[action] Her tether allows her to go to the edge of the platform. She 
[action] bravely lurches OVER THE EDGE -- standing at a 45 degree 
[action] angle now -- continues firing -- BLAM! BLAM! BLAM!

[action] Forcing the injured Alien to take cover in the port-side 
[action] engine.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] (CALLS UP)
[dialogue] TENNESSEE! PORT ENGINE!

[action] IN THE CAB :

[action] On his monitor, Tennessee has seen the ALIEN take refuge in 
[action] the engine--

[action] He slams the ENGINE CONTROLS--

[action] ON THE PLATFORM :

[action] THE PORT ENGINE BLASTS FIRE!

[action] THE CARGO LIFT LURCHES UP AS--

[action] THE ALIEN IS BLOWN OUT OF THE ENGINE--

[action] AND FALLS AWAY TO THE GROUND 100 FEET BELOW.

[action] Daniels FALLS in the violent lurch of the ship--

[action] But the tether holds. She SWINGS free like a pendulum. 
[action] Dangling.

[action] Until the Cargo Lift rights itself.

[action] She climbs back up to the topside of the platform. Collapses 
[action] in exhaustion.
