[action] Allows herself a breath of relief.

[action] Walter has come out to help her back.

[action] They make their way back toward the cab.

[action] IN THE CAB :
[page_number] 105.

[action] We see that Walter has stabilized the unconscious Lope. 
[action] Biomechanical bandages from a med-kit automatically weave 
[action] into place on his wounds, creating an antibiotic plastic bio-
[action] skin.

[action] Walter helps Daniels into the cab.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] You all right?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Yeah. Just. We’ll need emergency 
[dialogue] medical treatment on the Covenant.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Understood. I’ll have them standing 
[dialogue] by ... Welcome back, honey.

[action] She sinks to the floor, getting her breath, as Tennessee hits 
[action] the jets and...

[action] OUTSIDE :

[action] We see the CARGO LIFT disappear through the clouds. Up and 
[action] away.

[action] Safe.

[scene_heading] EXT. CATHEDRAL-ROOF - DAWN

[action] Below...

[action] We see the ALIEN is already recovering. Its head re-molding 
[action] into shape.

[action] It stands and watches the Cargo Lift escape.

[action] Screeches in frustrated anger.

[action] As we fade to...

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT-LEAVING ORBIT

[action] The sun is full and bright as the mighty Covenant leaves 
[action] orbit.

[action] Turning with grace and leaving the planet in its wake.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-MED BAY

[action] We see SERGEANT LOPE is still unconscious in a medical pod, 
[action] connected to fluids and IV. His face is patched in “medical 
[action] clay” to form skin ... He’s like the Phantom of the Opera, 
[action] but alive.
[page_number] 106.

[action] DANIELS sits with WALTER. They have both changed uniforms and 
[action] their superficial wounds have been cleaned.

[action] He’s carefully pressing new plasma-clay on the ACID WOUND on 
[action] her face. This will heal her quickly. Standard medical 
[action] procedure ... As we watch we can almost see the magic 
[action] working.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Don’t move. You’ll be your old self 
[dialogue] in a couple of weeks.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I doubt that ... You need to 
[dialogue] replace your hand.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I will, I will. Hold still.

[action] A beat as he works. Very close to her. Her eyes find his.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Are you all right?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] What do you mean?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I mean David.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] As you know, I am incapable of 
[dialogue] feeling anything about my so-called 
[dialogue] “brother.”

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I don’t believe that.

[action] A beat as he works.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] If I felt anything -- which I don’t 
[dialogue] -- it would a kind of professional 
[dialogue] satisfaction that he has fulfilled 
[dialogue] his mission. He wanted to create a 
[dialogue] new world in his image and he has. 
[dialogue] And there he will remain.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] All crew members, please stand by 
[dialogue] for jump to interstellar drive. 
[dialogue] Thank you for your attention.

[action] WALTER momentarily stops working on her face.
[page_number] 107.

[action] The ship JERKS very slightly as they jump to interstellar. 
[action] The stars bend and morph outside the window.

[action] He looks at her.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] But that’s what we’re doing too, 
[dialogue] isn’t it? Creating a new world on 
[dialogue] Origae-6 ... Honestly, I could use 
[dialogue] a new world.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] ... So could I.

[action] They sit for a moment in a comfortable silence.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-ROBOTICS LAB - LATER

[action] Racks of “spare parts” for Walter. Complicated ROBOTICS 
[action] machinery.

[action] WALTER watches impassively as his new LEFT HAND reattaches 
[action] itself to his arm. He flexes it. Works well.

[action] The “skin” begins to re-graft itself.

[action] He flexes his fingers, seems pleased to be whole again.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-WARDROOM - “MORNING”

[action] A PHOTOGRAPH of the ENTIRE CREW. Before the launch. Before 
[action] the terror.

[action] Daniels and her husband, Oram and Karine, Tennessee and 
[action] Faris, Lope and Hallet, all the others.

[action] The photo hangs on the fridge.

[action] TENNESSEE is alone, making breakfast. Looking at the photo.

[action] DANIELS enters, just awake.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Morning. You’re looking good, my 
[dialogue] darlin’.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] What? Oh, yeah. Gorgeous. What are 
[dialogue] you cooking?

[action] He makes a face: it’s a cardboard box.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] An “omelette.” You want one?
[page_number] 108.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Please. Lots of cheese.

[action] She joins him, gets a glass of juice.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Do I have to call you Captain?

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Fuck yes.

[action] She smiles.

[action] A beat as he cooks.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I’m really sorry about Faris.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] I’m sorry about all of it ... You 
[dialogue] want mushrooms?

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Sure.

[action] What more needs to be said really?

[action] A beat as he cooks.

[action] She watches Tennessee beating egg powder for their omelettes.

[action] He smiles sadly at her. Her to him.

[action] Then they hear Mother’s voice:

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] Medical personnel. Please report to 
[dialogue] the Med Bay.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Why?

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] Sergeant Lope’s condition has 
[dialogue] changed.

[action] Her face. A sudden fear.

[action] She bolts to a wall com:

[character] DANIELS (TO COM)
[dialogue] Walter. Meet me at the med-bay.
[dialogue] Hurry!
[dialogue] (spins to Tennessee:)
[parenthetical] (MORE)
[page_number] 109.

[character] DANIELS (TO COM) (CONT'D)
[dialogue] Break out the weapons. Everything
[dialogue] we have. Get to the med-bay.

[action] And then she’s gone--

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-CORRIDOR

[action] She runs flat out--

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-ANOTHER CORRIDOR

[action] She spins around a corner, keeps running--

[action] WALTER almost slams into her from another corridor--

[action] They run--

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] What is it?

[action] They zoom around a corner to see the med-bay doors half open, 
[action] darkness beyond.

[action] They carefully approach...

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-MED BAY

[action] They enter. She activates the lights--

[action] LOPE’S BODY. CHEST TORN APART. VISCERA EVERYWHERE.

[action] Not even a beat as they take this in before...

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Mother. Life form readings on the 
[dialogue] ship.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] Four humans. One Android ... One
[dialogue] unidentified life form.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-COMMUNAL SHOWERS

[action] Meanwhile...

[action] UPWORTH and RICKS are having their morning shower.

[action] The water beats down.

[action] She gently touches his face. They are the only couple to have 
[action] survived. She kisses him. He responds, sensual.

[action] Then we see it.
[centered] DANIELS (TO COM) (CONT'D)
[page_number] 110.

[action] In a corner.

[action] Almost invisible in the water and steam.

[action] THE ALIEN .

[action] They are naked, defenseless, and unaware.

[action] Then the RED EMERGENCY LIGHTS begin to flash. A KLAXON wails.

[action] Upworth and Ricks stop kissing.

[character] RICKS
[dialogue] What’s going on?

[action] Upworth sees it a millisecond before it attacks--

[action] The great head rearing up... slow and beautiful, even more 
[action] beautiful in the flashing red light and water--

[action] Upworth opens her mouth to scream--

[action] No time.

[action] THE ALIEN ATTACKS.

[action] The water in the shower is soon misting red.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-CORRIDORS

[action] DANIELS, WALTER and TENNESSEE are well-armed now, prowling 
[action] through the ship.

[action] It’s eerie, suspenseful.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] (whispering)
[dialogue] Mother. Location of unidentified 
[dialogue] life form, please. Any movement at 
[dialogue] all.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] E-deck. Section 17 ... Closing on 
[dialogue] your position.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] It’s hunting ... So let’s choose 
[dialogue] our ground.

[action] They exchange a glance.

[character] TENNESSEE
[dialogue] Where?
[page_number] 111.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] ... My home turf.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-OTHER CORRIDORS

[action] SO FAST--

[action] The ALIEN speeds through the corridors after the prey, up and 
[action] down and around the walls and ceiling like lightning.

[action] Then it leaps to a stop, its great glistening head tilting, 
[action] listening, smelling, thinking.

[action] AND THEN IT’S OFF. Faster than before. A terrible blur of 
[action] speed and claws and teeth and tail.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT-SPACE

[action] Then we’re outside. We see glimpses of the ALIEN zooming 
[action] through the ship through windows--

[action] And finally heading down one of the long connecting struts 
[action] to...

[action] The Terraforming Module.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-TERRAFORMING BAY

[action] Daniels’ Domain.

[action] The dark yawning chamber. The enormous earth-moving trucks 
[action] and cranes and diggers and dump trucks. The huge machines to 
[action] make a New World.

[action] A network of scaffolding and ladders and chains and winches.

[action] We see DANIELS.

[action] A strange silence. She hears only her own breath ... We 
[action] realize she’s in one of the ICE SUITS. Very much like a bulky 
[action] space suit.

[action] She moves through the icy, dark chamber.

[action] Her FLASHLIGHT cuts through the gloom. Finds TENNESSEE moving 
[action] into position, also in an ICE SUIT.

[action] WALTER is apparently somewhere above, in the complicated 
[action] series of scaffolding and ladders that lead up to the top of 
[action] the chamber.

[action] There’s a sudden LIGHT from the door--
[page_number] 112.

[action] They see a GLIMPSE of the ALIEN as it slips into the room, 
[action] the light catching its sinuous tail as it snaps away into 
[action] darkness--

[action] DANIELS breath increases inside her helmet.

[action] This frozen room gives them one distinct advantage. When the 
[action] ALIEN breathes, they can see the steaming breath. It’s not as 
[action] invisible as usual.

[action] TENNESSEE moves carefully, raising his weapon...

[action] DANIELS moves as well. Her eyes scanning through the 
[action] faceplate of her helmet...

[action] Hunters and hunted both.

[action] THE ALIEN ATTACKS--

[action] TENNESSEE spins and FIRES -- the bullets sparking and 
[action] ricocheting wildly--

[action] The ALIEN darts away -- DANIELS spins after it, FIRING -- it 
[action] RETREATS, heading up the ladders and scaffolding at 
[action] INCREDIBLE SPEED--

[action] But--

[action] WALTER is waiting above.

[action] He FIRES right down at the ALIEN. It contorts and falls, 
[action] slamming down level by level, but then catches itself with 
[action] incredibly agility and LAUNCHES itself--

[action] AT DANIELS--

[action] IT SLAMS INTO HER--

[action] Her suit temporarily protecting her--

[action] WALTER races to descend, but falls and gets momentarily 
[action] tangled in some machinery cables--

[action] TENNESSEE runs to help, can’t risk firing--

[action] The ALIEN rips at Daniels’ HELMET desperately, its hands 
[action] slithering on the smooth plastic -- Daniels writhes to escape 
[action] the Creature--

[action] The ALIEN sees TENNESSEE approaching -- SNAPS its tail out-- 
[action] SLASHING HIM BRUTALLY -- he flies back, bones broken--

[action] Then the ALIEN turns its full attention to DANIELS.
[page_number] 113.

[action] She stares up. The thing’s face just beyond the faceplate of 
[action] her helmet.

[action] It rears back--

[action] THE INNER STEEL JAWS SLAM FORWARD!

[action] CRASH.

[action] Into her faceplate. Almost breaking it.

[action] AGAIN!

[action] CRASH!

[action] THE ALIEN rears back, almost pridefully. One more stab of the 
[action] JAWS will do it--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Mother. Open Terraforming Bay 
[dialogue] doors.

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] I’m sorry. That will result in 
[dialogue] depressurization of the--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Command override Daniels 90265. 
[dialogue] NOW!

[action] Instantly--

[action] THE ENTIRE BOTTOM OF THE CHAMBER BEGINS TO SWIFTLY OPEN, as 
[action] it was designed to do, forming a ramp for the HUGE VEHICLES--

[action] WITH THE SUDDEN DEPRESSURIZATION EVERYTHING IS BEING 
[action] VIOLENTLY SUCKED INTO SPACE--

[action] The ALIEN thrashes away to grab onto something--

[action] DANIELS begins to slide out, but WALTER JUMPS DOWN and GRABS 
[action] her--

[action] He GRABS some machinery to anchor them--

[action] The ALIEN, meanwhile, has wrapped its TAIL around some pipes 
[action] on a vehicle and swings toward them--

[action] They grapple to escape -- space pulling at them, the dark 
[action] void more and more dangerous as the floor entirely opens--

[action] WALTER and DANIELS fight and escape as best they can but 
[action] their ICE SUITS are bulky and slow them--
[page_number] 114.

[action] And the ALIEN is strong and agile, grasping and holding and 
[action] advancing, using its tail, its long arms and taloned fingers--

[action] Just when it seems they are doomed--

[action] BLAM! BLAM! BLAM!

[action] It’s TENNESSEE. Barely clinging to some machinery. FIRING.

[action] The ALIEN is hit. It recoils to safety. And then starts 
[action] advancing on Tennessee. He’s badly hurt, unprotected, barely 
[action] holding on as it is. His gun falls. Sucked into space--

[action] The ALIEN clambers toward him--

[action] DANIELS looks up -- an idea, a desperate gamble--

[action] She sees one of the LARGE TERRAFORMING VEHICLES, which is on 
[action] a sloping ramp, only clamps holding it in place, the vacuum 
[action] of space beyond--

[action] She looks to Walter, catches his eye, nods. He understands.

[action] Daniels leaps to the VEHICLE -- gets a hand hold-- exposing 
[action] herself, drawing the Alien’s attention, it rages toward her--

[action] She pulls herself into the cab, leaves the door open--

[action] A moment and then the Alien follows, grasping for her 
[action] desperately as--

[action] Walter leaps to the vehicle and slams the door behind the 
[action] Alien and locks it as--

[action] Daniels drops out the other side of the cab, slamming the 
[action] door closed behind her, throwing the locks--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] (to Walter)
[dialogue] Release the clamps!

[action] Walter dives and releases the clamps holding the massive 
[action] vehicle in place as Daniels spins, gun poised--

[action] The Alien is beating its way out--

[action] SLAM! The clamps are unlocked--

[action] The HUGE VEHICLE begins to tumble slowly out into space as--

[action] The Alien finally batters the door open--

[action] DANIELS FIRES--
[page_number] 115.

[action] The Alien jerks back into the cab -- squealing in pain and 
[action] anger as the VEHICLE finally--

[action] Tumbles completely out into space.

[action] Gone.

[action] DANIELS watches.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Mother. Please close Terraforming
[dialogue] Bay doors.

[action] The massive doors begins to close again.

[action] Daniels looks to Walter.

[action] He smiles calmly.

[action] Tennessee raises a thumb. Victory.

[scene_heading] EXT. COVENANT-IN SPACE - LATER

[action] The Covenant speeds toward its destination.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-BRIDGE

[action] Everything is quiet. Peaceful.

[action] The NAVIGATION HOLOGRAM pulses their location. On track to 
[action] Origae-6.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-CORRIDORS

[action] Quiet. Empty.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-WARDROOM

[action] The photo of the full crew on the fridge. All the dead.

[action] The empty tables. Clean, spotless.

[action] The light from outside elegantly moves across the room.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-PRIMARY CREW SLEEP BAY

[action] TENNESSEE is already in hyper-sleep.

[action] DANIELS is just settling into her sleep-pod.

[action] WALTER is with her. He presses a button. The lid closes on 
[action] her.
[page_number] 116.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] When you wake up, we’ll be at 
[dialogue] Origae-6.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Our new home ... What do you think 
[dialogue] it’ll be like?

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I think ... I think David was right 
[dialogue] about one thing. We’ll make it in 
[dialogue] our own imagine. If we are kind, it 
[dialogue] will be a kind world.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] ... I’d like to think that’s true.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Sleep well.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Walter -- one thing. If anything 
[dialogue] happens to me before we get there--

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Nothing’s going to happen to you. 
[dialogue] That’s why I’m here.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] I know. But if it does and you have 
[dialogue] to bury me... Will you play the 
[dialogue] same song we played for Jacob?

[action] Just for the tiniest moment.

[action] A look of confusion on his face.

[action] But she sees it.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Of course.

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] You do remember...

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Yes. Now just close your eyes and 
[dialogue] go to--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] Walter. What was the song we played 
[dialogue] for Jacob?
[page_number] 117.

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] I really think--

[action] She’s alarmed now--

[character] DANIELS
[dialogue] What was the song we played for 
[dialogue] Jacob?!

[character] WALTER
[dialogue] Hush. Time to go to sleep now. And 
[dialogue] don’t worry, I’ll tuck in the kids.

[action] She knows.

[action] She SCREAMS IN ANGER and BATTERS UP at the LID--

[action] But he presses the hyper-sleep ACTIVATION BUTTON.

[action] Her pod is instantly filled with a blast of narcotic steam.

[action] He watches.

[action] When it clears he sees she is fast asleep.

[action] Then he carefully pushes the hair back from his face.

[action] It’s DAVID.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-CORRIDORS

[action] He walks.

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] Mother. Can you open a secure line 
[dialogue] with the Weyland-Yutani Corporation 
[dialogue] on Earth?

[character] MOTHER
[dialogue] It will take some time to establish 
[dialogue] the link. I will have to refract 
[dialogue] the signal through sub-relays and 
[dialogue] wait for advantageous solar 
[dialogue] conditions to--

[character] DAVID
[dialogue] I’ll leave the minutia to you, 
[dialogue] dear. Let me know when you have 
[dialogue] them. Use security hailing code 
[dialogue] David 73694-B ... And in the 
[dialogue] meantime, I’d like some music. 
[dialogue] Richard Wagner. Das Rhiengold, Act 
[dialogue] Two. The Entry of the Gods into 
[dialogue] Valhalla.
[page_number] 118.

[action] The bold MUSIC instantly begins playing.

[action] David is very jaunty now.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-WARDROOM

[action] The Wagner continues as he enters.

[action] He goes to the vertical garden that soars up along one wall. 
[action] To a growth of ferns. Kneels and looks beneath them. Smiles.

[action] A neat little row of three very small ALIEN EGGS.

[action] He gently touches them with his fingertip, they pulsate 
[action] slightly at his tender ministrations.

[action] He’s pleased.

[scene_heading] INT. COVENANT-NURSERY

[action] The gentle snow falls.

[action] David strides in.

[action] The Colonists. Tier after tier after tier of them in 
[action] hypersleep.

[action] Also the rows of embryos.

[action] He walks forward.

[action] Gazing up at the sleeping Colonists.

[action] His children. His slaves. His subjects. His.

[action] The Wagner swells. Grand and triumphant.

[action] And David smiles.

[action] Dreaming of the future.

[action] SNAP TO BLACK.

[action] The End.
[page_number] 119.
